english: And Reuben heard it, and delivered him out of their hand; and said: ‘Let us not take his life.’

searchText: genesis 37:22 and reuben said unto them: ‘shed no blood; cast him into this pit that is in the wilderness, but lay no hand upon him’—that he might deliver him out of their hand, to restore him to his father.
english: And Reuben said unto them: ‘Shed no blood; cast him into this pit that is in the wilderness, but lay no hand upon him’—that he might deliver him out of their hand, to restore him to his father.

searchText: genesis 37:23 and it came to pass, when joseph was come unto his brethren, that they stripped joseph of his coat, the coat of many colours that was on him;
english: And it came to pass, when Joseph was come unto his brethren, that they stripped Joseph of his coat, the coat of many colours that was on him;

searchText: genesis 37:24 and they took him, and cast him into the pit—and the pit was empty, there was no water in it.
english: and they took him, and cast him into the pit—and the pit was empty, there was no water in it.

searchText: genesis 37:25 and they sat down to eat bread; and they lifted up their eyes and looked, and, behold, a caravan of ishmaelites came from gilead, with their camels bearing spicery and balm and ladanum, going to carry it down to egypt.
english: And they sat down to eat bread; and they lifted up their eyes and looked, and, behold, a caravan of Ishmaelites came from Gilead, with their camels bearing spicery and balm and ladanum, going to carry it down to Egypt.

searchText: genesis 37:26 and judah said unto his brethren: ‘what profit is it if we slay our brother and conceal his blood?
english: And Judah said unto his brethren: ‘What profit is it if we slay our brother and conceal his blood?

searchText: genesis 37:27 come, and let us sell him to the ishmaelites, and let not our hand be upon him; for he is our brother, our flesh.’ and his brethren hearkened unto him.
english: Come, and let us sell him to the Ishmaelites, and let not our hand be upon him; for he is our brother, our flesh.’ And his brethren hearkened unto him.

searchText: genesis 37:28 and there passed by midianites, merchantmen; and they drew and lifted up joseph out of the pit, and sold joseph to the ishmaelites for twenty shekels of silver. and they brought joseph into egypt.
english: And there passed by Midianites, merchantmen; and they drew and lifted up Joseph out of the pit, and sold Joseph to the Ishmaelites for twenty shekels of silver. And they brought Joseph into Egypt.

searchText: genesis 37:29 and reuben returned unto the pit; and, behold, joseph was not in the pit; and he rent his clothes.
english: And Reuben returned unto the pit; and, behold, Joseph was not in the pit; and he rent his clothes.

searchText: genesis 37:30 and he returned unto his brethren, and said: ‘the child is not; and as for me, whither shall i go?’
english: And he returned unto his brethren, and said: ‘The child is not; and as for me, whither shall I go?’

searchText: genesis 37:31 and they took joseph’s coat, and killed a he-goat, and dipped the coat in the blood;
english: And they took Joseph’s coat, and killed a he-goat, and dipped the coat in the blood;

searchText: genesis 37:32 and they sent the coat of many colours, and they brought it to their father; and said: ‘this have we found. know now whether it is thy son’s coat or not.’
english: and they sent the coat of many colours, and they brought it to their father; and said: ‘This have we found. Know now whether it is thy son’s coat or not.’

searchText: genesis 37:33 and he knew it, and said: ‘it is my son’s coat; an evil beast hath devoured him; joseph is without doubt torn in pieces.’
english: And he knew it, and said: ‘It is my son’s coat; an evil beast hath devoured him; Joseph is without doubt torn in pieces.’

searchText: genesis 37:34 and jacob rent his garments, and put sackcloth upon his loins, and mourned for his son many days.
english: And Jacob rent his garments, and put sackcloth upon his loins, and mourned for his son many days.

searchText: genesis 37:35 and all his sons and all his daughters rose up to comfort him; but he refused to be comforted; and he said: ‘nay, but i will go down to the grave to my son mourning.’ and his father wept for him.
english: And all his sons and all his daughters rose up to comfort him; but he refused to be comforted; and he said: ‘Nay, but I will go down to the grave to my son mourning.’ And his father wept for him.

searchText: genesis 37:36 and the midianites sold him into egypt unto potiphar, an officer of pharaoh’s, the captain of the guard.
english: And the Midianites sold him into Egypt unto Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh’s, the captain of the guard.

searchText: genesis 38:1 and it came to pass at that time, that judah went down from his brethren, and turned in to a certain adullamite, whose name was hirah.
english: And it came to pass at that time, that Judah went down from his brethren, and turned in to a certain Adullamite, whose name was Hirah.

searchText: genesis 38:2 and judah saw there a daughter of a certain canaanite whose name was shua; and he took her, and went in unto her.
english: And Judah saw there a daughter of a certain Canaanite whose name was Shua; and he took her, and went in unto her.

searchText: genesis 38:3 and she conceived, and bore a son; and he called his name er.
english: And she conceived, and bore a son; and he called his name Er.

searchText: genesis 38:4 and she conceived again, and bore a son; and she called his name onan.
english: And she conceived again, and bore a son; and she called his name Onan.

searchText: genesis 38:5 and she yet again bore a son, and called his name shelah; and he was at chezib, when she bore him.
english: And she yet again bore a son, and called his name Shelah; and he was at Chezib, when she bore him.

searchText: genesis 38:6 and judah took a wife for er his first-born, and her name was tamar.
english: And Judah took a wife for Er his first-born, and her name was Tamar.

searchText: genesis 38:7 and er, judah’s first-born, was wicked in the sight of the lord; and the lord slew him.
english: And Er, Judah’s first-born, was wicked in the sight of the LORD; and the LORD slew him.

searchText: genesis 38:8 and judah said unto onan: ‘go in unto thy brother’s wife, and perform the duty of a husband’s brother unto her, and raise up seed to thy brother.’
english: And Judah said unto Onan: ‘Go in unto thy brother’s wife, and perform the duty of a husband’s brother unto her, and raise up seed to thy brother.’

searchText: genesis 38:9 and onan knew that the seed would not be his; and it came to pass when he went in unto his brother’s wife, that he spilled it on the ground, lest he should give seed to his brother.
english: And Onan knew that the seed would not be his; and it came to pass when he went in unto his brother’s wife, that he spilled it on the ground, lest he should give seed to his brother.

searchText: genesis 38:10 and the thing which he did was evil in the sight of the lord; and he slew him also.
english: And the thing which he did was evil in the sight of the LORD; and He slew him also.

searchText: genesis 38:11 then said judah to tamar his daughter-in-law: ‘remain a widow in thy father’s house, till shelah my son be grown up’; for he said: ‘lest he also die, like his brethren.’ and tamar went and dwelt in her father’s house.
english: Then said Judah to Tamar his daughter-in-law: ‘Remain a widow in thy father’s house, till Shelah my son be grown up’; for he said: ‘Lest he also die, like his brethren.’ And Tamar went and dwelt in her father’s house.

searchText: genesis 38:12 and in process of time shua’s daughter, the wife of judah, died; and judah was comforted, and went up unto his sheep-shearers to timnah, he and his friend hirah the adullamite.
english: And in process of time Shua’s daughter, the wife of Judah, died; and Judah was comforted, and went up unto his sheep-shearers to Timnah, he and his friend Hirah the Adullamite.

searchText: genesis 38:13 and it was told tamar, saying: ‘behold, thy father-in-law goeth up to timnah to shear his sheep.’
english: And it was told Tamar, saying: ‘Behold, thy father-in-law goeth up to Timnah to shear his sheep.’

searchText: genesis 38:14 and she put off from her the garments of her widowhood, and covered herself with her veil, and wrapped herself, and sat in the entrance of enaim, which is by the way to timnah; for she saw that shelah was grown up, and she was not given unto him to wife.
english: And she put off from her the garments of her widowhood, and covered herself with her veil, and wrapped herself, and sat in the entrance of Enaim, which is by the way to Timnah; for she saw that Shelah was grown up, and she was not given unto him to wife.

searchText: genesis 38:15 when judah saw her, he thought her to be a harlot; for she had covered her face.
english: When Judah saw her, he thought her to be a harlot; for she had covered her face.

searchText: genesis 38:16 and he turned unto her by the way, and said: ‘come, i pray thee, let me come in unto thee’; for he knew not that she was his daughter-in-law. and she said: ‘what wilt thou give me, that thou mayest come in unto me?’
english: And he turned unto her by the way, and said: ‘Come, I pray thee, let me come in unto thee’; for he knew not that she was his daughter-in-law. And she said: ‘What wilt thou give me, that thou mayest come in unto me?’

searchText: genesis 38:17 and he said: ‘i will send thee a kid of the goats from the flock.’ and she said: ‘wilt thou give me a pledge, till thou send it?’
english: And he said: ‘I will send thee a kid of the goats from the flock.’ And she said: ‘Wilt thou give me a pledge, till thou send it?’

searchText: genesis 38:18 and he said: ‘what pledge shall i give thee?’ and she said: ‘thy signet and thy cord, and thy staff that is in thy hand.’ and he gave them to her, and came in unto her, and she conceived by him.
english: And he said: ‘What pledge shall I give thee?’ And she said: ‘Thy signet and thy cord, and thy staff that is in thy hand.’ And he gave them to her, and came in unto her, and she conceived by him.

searchText: genesis 38:19 and she arose, and went away, and put off her veil from her, and put on the garments of her widowhood.
english: And she arose, and went away, and put off her veil from her, and put on the garments of her widowhood.

searchText: genesis 38:20 and judah sent the kid of the goats by the hand of his friend the adullamite, to receive the pledge from the woman’s hand; but he found her not.
english: And Judah sent the kid of the goats by the hand of his friend the Adullamite, to receive the pledge from the woman’s hand; but he found her not.

searchText: genesis 38:21 then he asked the men of her place, saying: ‘where is the harlot, that was at enaim by the wayside?’ and they said: ‘there hath been no harlot here.’
english: Then he asked the men of her place, saying: ‘Where is the harlot, that was at Enaim by the wayside?’ And they said: ‘There hath been no harlot here.’

searchText: genesis 38:22 and he returned to judah, and said: ‘i have not found her; and also the men of the place said: there hath been no harlot here.’
english: And he returned to Judah, and said: ‘I have not found her; and also the men of the place said: There hath been no harlot here.’

searchText: genesis 38:23 and judah said: ‘let her take it, lest we be put to shame; behold, i sent this kid, and thou hast not found her.’
english: And Judah said: ‘Let her take it, lest we be put to shame; behold, I sent this kid, and thou hast not found her.’

searchText: genesis 38:24 and it came to pass about three months after, that it was told judah, saying: ‘tamar thy daughter-in-law hath played the harlot; and moreover, behold, she is with child by harlotry.’ and judah said: ‘bring her forth, and let her be burnt.’
english: And it came to pass about three months after, that it was told Judah, saying: ‘Tamar thy daughter-in-law hath played the harlot; and moreover, behold, she is with child by harlotry.’ And Judah said: ‘Bring her forth, and let her be burnt.’

searchText: genesis 38:25 when she was brought forth, she sent to her father-in-law, saying: ‘by the man, whose these are, am i with child’; and she said: ‘discern, i pray thee, whose are these, the signet, and the cords, and the staff.’
english: When she was brought forth, she sent to her father-in-law, saying: ‘By the man, whose these are, am I with child’; and she said: ‘Discern, I pray thee, whose are these, the signet, and the cords, and the staff.’

searchText: genesis 38:26 and judah acknowledged them, and said: ‘she is more righteous than i; forasmuch as i gave her not to shelah my son.’ and he knew her again no more.
english: And Judah acknowledged them, and said: ‘She is more righteous than I; forasmuch as I gave her not to Shelah my son.’ And he knew her again no more.

searchText: genesis 38:27 and it came to pass in the time of her travail, that, behold, twins were in her womb.
english: And it came to pass in the time of her travail, that, behold, twins were in her womb.

searchText: genesis 38:28 and it came to pass, when she travailed, that one put out a hand; and the midwife took and bound upon his hand a scarlet thread, saying: ‘this came out first.’
english: And it came to pass, when she travailed, that one put out a hand; and the midwife took and bound upon his hand a scarlet thread, saying: ‘This came out first.’

searchText: genesis 38:29 and it came to pass, as he drew back his hand, that, behold his brother came out; and she said: ‘wherefore hast thou made a breach for thyself?’ therefore his name was called perez.
english: And it came to pass, as he drew back his hand, that, behold his brother came out; and she said: ‘Wherefore hast thou made a breach for thyself?’ Therefore his name was called Perez.

searchText: genesis 38:30 and afterward came out his brother, that had the scarlet thread upon his hand; and his name was called zerah.
english: And afterward came out his brother, that had the scarlet thread upon his hand; and his name was called Zerah.

searchText: genesis 39:1 and joseph was brought down to egypt; and potiphar, an officer of pharaoh’s, the captain of the guard, an egyptian, bought him of the hand of the ishmaelites, that had brought him down thither.
english: And Joseph was brought down to Egypt; and Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh’s, the captain of the guard, an Egyptian, bought him of the hand of the Ishmaelites, that had brought him down thither.

searchText: genesis 39:2 and the lord was with joseph, and he was a prosperous man; and he was in the house of his master the egyptian.
english: And the LORD was with Joseph, and he was a prosperous man; and he was in the house of his master the Egyptian.

searchText: genesis 39:3 and his master saw that the lord was with him, and that the lord made all that he did to prosper in his hand.
english: And his master saw that the LORD was with him, and that the LORD made all that he did to prosper in his hand.

searchText: genesis 39:4 and joseph found favour in his sight, and he ministered unto him. and he appointed him overseer over his house, and all that he had he put into his hand.
english: And Joseph found favour in his sight, and he ministered unto him. And he appointed him overseer over his house, and all that he had he put into his hand.

searchText: genesis 39:5 and it came to pass from the time that he appointed him overseer in his house, and over all that he had, that the lord blessed the egyptian’s house for joseph’s sake; and the blessing of the lord was upon all that he had, in the house and in the field.
english: And it came to pass from the time that he appointed him overseer in his house, and over all that he had, that the LORD blessed the Egyptian’s house for Joseph’s sake; and the blessing of the LORD was upon all that he had, in the house and in the field.

searchText: genesis 39:6 and he left all that he had in joseph’s hand; and, having him, he knew not aught save the bread which he did eat. and joseph was of beautiful form, and fair to look upon.
english: And he left all that he had in Joseph’s hand; and, having him, he knew not aught save the bread which he did eat. And Joseph was of beautiful form, and fair to look upon.

searchText: genesis 39:7 and it came to pass after these things, that his master’s wife cast her eyes upon joseph; and she said: ‘lie with me.’
english: And it came to pass after these things, that his master’s wife cast her eyes upon Joseph; and she said: ‘Lie with me.’

searchText: genesis 39:8 but he refused, and said unto his master’s wife: ‘behold, my master, having me, knoweth not what is in the house, and he hath put all that he hath into my hand;
english: But he refused, and said unto his master’s wife: ‘Behold, my master, having me, knoweth not what is in the house, and he hath put all that he hath into my hand;

searchText: genesis 39:9 he is not greater in this house than i; neither hath he kept back any thing from me but thee, because thou art his wife. how then can i do this great wickedness, and sin against god?’
english: he is not greater in this house than I; neither hath he kept back any thing from me but thee, because thou art his wife. How then can I do this great wickedness, and sin against God?’

searchText: genesis 39:10 and it came to pass, as she spoke to joseph day by day, that he hearkened not unto her, to lie by her, or to be with her.
english: And it came to pass, as she spoke to Joseph day by day, that he hearkened not unto her, to lie by her, or to be with her.

searchText: genesis 39:11 and it came to pass on a certain day, when he went into the house to do his work, and there was none of the men of the house there within,
english: And it came to pass on a certain day, when he went into the house to do his work, and there was none of the men of the house there within,

searchText: genesis 39:12 that she caught him by his garment, saying: ‘lie with me.’ and he left his garment in her hand, and fled, and got him out.
english: that she caught him by his garment, saying: ‘Lie with me.’ And he left his garment in her hand, and fled, and got him out.

searchText: genesis 39:13 and it came to pass, when she saw that he had left his garment in her hand, and was fled forth,
english: And it came to pass, when she saw that he had left his garment in her hand, and was fled forth,

searchText: genesis 39:14 that she called unto the men of her house, and spoke unto them, saying: ‘see, he hath brought in a hebrew unto us to mock us; he came in unto me to lie with me, and i cried with a loud voice.
english: that she called unto the men of her house, and spoke unto them, saying: ‘See, he hath brought in a Hebrew unto us to mock us; he came in unto me to lie with me, and I cried with a loud voice.

searchText: genesis 39:15 and it came to pass, when he heard that i lifted up my voice and cried, that he left his garment by me, and fled, and got him out.’
english: And it came to pass, when he heard that I lifted up my voice and cried, that he left his garment by me, and fled, and got him out.’

searchText: genesis 39:16 and she laid up his garment by her, until his master came home.
english: And she laid up his garment by her, until his master came home.

searchText: genesis 39:17 and she spoke unto him according to these words, saying: ‘the hebrew servant, whom thou hast brought unto us, came in unto me to mock me.
english: And she spoke unto him according to these words, saying: ‘The Hebrew servant, whom thou hast brought unto us, came in unto me to mock me.

searchText: genesis 39:18 and it came to pass, as i lifted up my voice and cried, that he left his garment by me, and fled out.’
english: And it came to pass, as I lifted up my voice and cried, that he left his garment by me, and fled out.’

searchText: genesis 39:19 and it came to pass, when his master heard the words of his wife, which she spoke unto him, saying: ‘after this manner did thy servant to me’; that his wrath was kindled.
english: And it came to pass, when his master heard the words of his wife, which she spoke unto him, saying: ‘After this manner did thy servant to me’; that his wrath was kindled.

searchText: genesis 39:20 and joseph’s master took him, and put him into the prison, the place where the king’s prisoners were bound; and he was there in the prison.
english: And Joseph’s master took him, and put him into the prison, the place where the king’s prisoners were bound; and he was there in the prison.

searchText: genesis 39:21 but the lord was with joseph, and showed kindness unto him, and gave him favour in the sight of the keeper of the prison.
english: But the LORD was with Joseph, and showed kindness unto him, and gave him favour in the sight of the keeper of the prison.

searchText: genesis 39:22 and the keeper of the prison committed to joseph’s hand all the prisoners that were in the prison; and whatsoever they did there, he was the doer of it.
english: And the keeper of the prison committed to Joseph’s hand all the prisoners that were in the prison; and whatsoever they did there, he was the doer of it.

searchText: genesis 39:23 the keeper of the prison looked not to any thing that was under his hand, because the lord was with him; and that which he did, the lord made it to prosper.
english: The keeper of the prison looked not to any thing that was under his hand, because the LORD was with him; and that which he did, the LORD made it to prosper.

searchText: genesis 40:1 and it came to pass after these things, that the butler of the king of egypt and his baker offended their lord the king of egypt.
english: And it came to pass after these things, that the butler of the king of Egypt and his baker offended their lord the king of Egypt.

searchText: genesis 40:2 and pharaoh was wroth against his two officers, against the chief of the butlers, and against the chief of the bakers.
english: And Pharaoh was wroth against his two officers, against the chief of the butlers, and against the chief of the bakers.

searchText: genesis 40:3 and he put them in ward in the house of the captain of the guard, into the prison, the place where joseph was bound.
english: And he put them in ward in the house of the captain of the guard, into the prison, the place where Joseph was bound.

searchText: genesis 40:4 and the captain of the guard charged joseph to be with them, and he ministered unto them; and they continued a season in ward.
english: And the captain of the guard charged Joseph to be with them, and he ministered unto them; and they continued a season in ward.

searchText: genesis 40:5 and they dreamed a dream both of them, each man his dream, in one night, each man according to the interpretation of his dream, the butler and the baker of the king of egypt, who were bound in the prison.
english: And they dreamed a dream both of them, each man his dream, in one night, each man according to the interpretation of his dream, the butler and the baker of the king of Egypt, who were bound in the prison.

searchText: genesis 40:6 and joseph came in unto them in the morning, and saw them, and, behold, they were sad.
english: And Joseph came in unto them in the morning, and saw them, and, behold, they were sad.

searchText: genesis 40:7 and he asked pharaoh’s officers that were with him in the ward of his master’s house, saying: ‘wherefore look ye so sad to-day?’
english: And he asked Pharaoh’s officers that were with him in the ward of his master’s house, saying: ‘Wherefore look ye so sad to-day?’

searchText: genesis 40:8 and they said unto him: ‘we have dreamed a dream, and there is none that can interpret it.’ and joseph said unto them: ‘do not interpretations belong to god? tell it me, i pray you.’
english: And they said unto him: ‘We have dreamed a dream, and there is none that can interpret it.’ And Joseph said unto them: ‘Do not interpretations belong to God? tell it me, I pray you.’

searchText: genesis 40:9 and the chief butler told his dream to joseph, and said to him: ‘in my dream, behold, a vine was before me;
english: And the chief butler told his dream to Joseph, and said to him: ‘In my dream, behold, a vine was before me;

searchText: genesis 40:10 and in the vine were three branches; and as it was budding, its blossoms shot forth, and the clusters thereof brought forth ripe grapes,
english: and in the vine were three branches; and as it was budding, its blossoms shot forth, and the clusters thereof brought forth ripe grapes,

searchText: genesis 40:11 and pharaoh’s cup was in my hand; and i took the grapes, and pressed them into pharaoh’s cup, and i gave the cup into pharaoh’s hand.’
english: and Pharaoh’s cup was in my hand; and I took the grapes, and pressed them into Pharaoh’s cup, and I gave the cup into Pharaoh’s hand.’

searchText: genesis 40:12 and joseph said unto him: ‘this is the interpretation of it: the three branches are three days;
english: And Joseph said unto him: ‘This is the interpretation of it: the three branches are three days;

searchText: genesis 40:13 within yet three days shall pharaoh lift up thy head, and restore thee unto thine office; and thou shalt give pharaoh’s cup into his hand, after the former manner when thou wast his butler.
english: within yet three days shall Pharaoh lift up thy head, and restore thee unto thine office; and thou shalt give Pharaoh’s cup into his hand, after the former manner when thou wast his butler.

searchText: genesis 40:14 but have me in thy remembrance when it shall be well with thee, and show kindness, i pray thee, unto me, and make mention of me unto pharaoh, and bring me out of this house.
english: But have me in thy remembrance when it shall be well with thee, and show kindness, I pray thee, unto me, and make mention of me unto Pharaoh, and bring me out of this house.

searchText: genesis 40:15 for indeed i was stolen away out of the land of the hebrews; and here also have i done nothing that they should put me into the dungeon.’
english: For indeed I was stolen away out of the land of the Hebrews; and here also have I done nothing that they should put me into the dungeon.’

searchText: genesis 40:16 when the chief baker saw that the interpretation was good, he said unto joseph: ‘i also saw in my dream, and, behold, three baskets of white bread were on my head;
english: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was good, he said unto Joseph: ‘I also saw in my dream, and, behold, three baskets of white bread were on my head;

searchText: genesis 40:17 and in the uppermost basket there was of all manner of baked food for pharaoh; and the birds did eat them out of the basket upon my head.’
english: and in the uppermost basket there was of all manner of baked food for Pharaoh; and the birds did eat them out of the basket upon my head.’

searchText: genesis 40:18 and joseph answered and said: ‘this is the interpretation thereof: the three baskets are three days;
english: And Joseph answered and said: ‘This is the interpretation thereof: the three baskets are three days;

searchText: genesis 40:19 within yet three days shall pharaoh lift up thy head from off thee, and shall hang thee on a tree; and the birds shall eat thy flesh from off thee.’
english: within yet three days shall Pharaoh lift up thy head from off thee, and shall hang thee on a tree; and the birds shall eat thy flesh from off thee.’

searchText: genesis 40:20 and it came to pass the third day, which was pharaoh’s birthday, that he made a feast unto all his servants; and he lifted up the head of the chief butler and the head of the chief baker among his servants.
english: And it came to pass the third day, which was Pharaoh’s birthday, that he made a feast unto all his servants; and he lifted up the head of the chief butler and the head of the chief baker among his servants.

searchText: genesis 40:21 and he restored the chief butler back unto his butlership; and he gave the cup into pharaoh’s hand.
english: And he restored the chief butler back unto his butlership; and he gave the cup into Pharaoh’s hand.

searchText: genesis 40:22 but he hanged the chief baker, as joseph had interpreted to them.
english: But he hanged the chief baker, as Joseph had interpreted to them.

searchText: genesis 40:23 yet did not the chief butler remember joseph, but forgot him.
english: Yet did not the chief butler remember Joseph, but forgot him.

searchText: genesis 41:1 and it came to pass at the end of two full years, that pharaoh dreamed: and, behold, he stood by the river.
english: And it came to pass at the end of two full years, that Pharaoh dreamed: and, behold, he stood by the river.

searchText: genesis 41:2 and, behold, there came up out of the river seven kine, well-favoured and fat-fleshed; and they fed in the reed-grass.
english: And, behold, there came up out of the river seven kine, well-favoured and fat-fleshed; and they fed in the reed-grass.

searchText: genesis 41:3 and, behold, seven other kine came up after them out of the river, ill favoured and lean-fleshed; and stood by the other kine upon the brink of the river.
english: And, behold, seven other kine came up after them out of the river, ill favoured and lean-fleshed; and stood by the other kine upon the brink of the river.

searchText: genesis 41:4 and the ill-favoured and lean-fleshed kine did eat up the seven well-favoured and fat kine. so pharaoh awoke.
english: And the ill-favoured and lean-fleshed kine did eat up the seven well-favoured and fat kine. So Pharaoh awoke.

searchText: genesis 41:5 and he slept and dreamed a second time: and, behold, seven ears of corn came up upon one stalk, rank and good.
english: And he slept and dreamed a second time: and, behold, seven ears of corn came up upon one stalk, rank and good.

searchText: genesis 41:6 and, behold, seven ears, thin and blasted with the east wind, sprung up after them.
english: And, behold, seven ears, thin and blasted with the east wind, sprung up after them.

searchText: genesis 41:7 and the thin ears swallowed up the seven rank and full ears. and pharaoh awoke, and, behold, it was a dream.
english: And the thin ears swallowed up the seven rank and full ears. And Pharaoh awoke, and, behold, it was a dream.

searchText: genesis 41:8 and it came to pass in the morning that his spirit was troubled; and he sent and called for all the magicians of egypt, and all the wise men thereof; and pharaoh told them his dream; but there was none that could interpret them unto pharaoh.
english: And it came to pass in the morning that his spirit was troubled; and he sent and called for all the magicians of Egypt, and all the wise men thereof; and Pharaoh told them his dream; but there was none that could interpret them unto Pharaoh.

searchText: genesis 41:9 then spoke the chief butler unto pharaoh, saying: ‘i make mention of my faults this day:
english: Then spoke the chief butler unto Pharaoh, saying: ‘I make mention of my faults this day:

searchText: genesis 41:10 pharaoh was wroth with his servants, and put me in the ward of the house of the captain of the guard, me and the chief baker.
english: Pharaoh was wroth with his servants, and put me in the ward of the house of the captain of the guard, me and the chief baker.

searchText: genesis 41:11 and we dreamed a dream in one night, i and he; we dreamed each man according to the interpretation of his dream.
english: And we dreamed a dream in one night, I and he; we dreamed each man according to the interpretation of his dream.

searchText: genesis 41:12 and there was with us there a young man, a hebrew, servant to the captain of the guard; and we told him, and he interpreted to us our dreams; to each man according to his dream he did interpret.
english: And there was with us there a young man, a Hebrew, servant to the captain of the guard; and we told him, and he interpreted to us our dreams; to each man according to his dream he did interpret.

searchText: genesis 41:13 and it came to pass, as he interpreted to us, so it was: i was restored unto mine office, and he was hanged.’
english: And it came to pass, as he interpreted to us, so it was: I was restored unto mine office, and he was hanged.’

searchText: genesis 41:14 then pharaoh sent and called joseph, and they brought him hastily out of the dungeon. and he shaved himself, and changed his raiment, and came in unto pharaoh.
english: Then Pharaoh sent and called Joseph, and they brought him hastily out of the dungeon. And he shaved himself, and changed his raiment, and came in unto Pharaoh.

searchText: genesis 41:15 and pharaoh said unto joseph: ‘i have dreamed a dream, and there is none that can interpret it; and i have heard say of thee, that when thou hearest a dream thou canst interpret it.’
english: And Pharaoh said unto Joseph: ‘I have dreamed a dream, and there is none that can interpret it; and I have heard say of thee, that when thou hearest a dream thou canst interpret it.’

searchText: genesis 41:16 and joseph answered pharaoh, saying: ‘it is not in me; god will give pharaoh an answer of peace.’
english: And Joseph answered Pharaoh, saying: ‘It is not in me; God will give Pharaoh an answer of peace.’

searchText: genesis 41:17 and pharaoh spoke unto joseph: ‘in my dream, behold, i stood upon the brink of the river.
english: And Pharaoh spoke unto Joseph: ‘In my dream, behold, I stood upon the brink of the river.

searchText: genesis 41:18 and, behold, there came up out of the river seven kine, fat-fleshed and well-favoured; and they fed in the reedgrass.
english: And, behold, there came up out of the river seven kine, fat-fleshed and well-favoured; and they fed in the reedgrass.

searchText: genesis 41:19 and, behold, seven other kine came up after them, poor and very ill-favoured and lean-fleshed, such as i never saw in all the land of egypt for badness.
english: And, behold, seven other kine came up after them, poor and very ill-favoured and lean-fleshed, such as I never saw in all the land of Egypt for badness.

searchText: genesis 41:20 and the lean and ill-favoured kine did eat up the first seven fat kine.
english: And the lean and ill-favoured kine did eat up the first seven fat kine.

searchText: genesis 41:21 and when they had eaten them up, it could not be known that they had eaten them; but they were still ill-favoured as at the beginning. so i awoke.
english: And when they had eaten them up, it could not be known that they had eaten them; but they were still ill-favoured as at the beginning. So I awoke.

searchText: genesis 41:22 and i saw in my dream, and, behold, seven ears came up upon one stalk, full and good.
english: And I saw in my dream, and, behold, seven ears came up upon one stalk, full and good.

searchText: genesis 41:23 and, behold, seven ears, withered, thin, and blasted with the east wind, sprung up after them.
english: And, behold, seven ears, withered, thin, and blasted with the east wind, sprung up after them.

searchText: genesis 41:24 and the thin ears swallowed up the seven good ears. and i told it unto the magicians; but there was none that could declare it to me.’
english: And the thin ears swallowed up the seven good ears. And I told it unto the magicians; but there was none that could declare it to me.’

searchText: genesis 41:25 and joseph said unto pharaoh: ‘the dream of pharaoh is one; what god is about to do he hath declared unto pharaoh.
english: And Joseph said unto Pharaoh: ‘The dream of Pharaoh is one; what God is about to do He hath declared unto Pharaoh.

searchText: genesis 41:26 the seven good kine are seven years; and the seven good ears are seven years: the dream is one.
english: The seven good kine are seven years; and the seven good ears are seven years: the dream is one.

searchText: genesis 41:27 and the seven lean and ill-favoured kine that came up after them are seven years, and also the seven empty ears blasted with the east wind; they shall be seven years of famine.
english: And the seven lean and ill-favoured kine that came up after them are seven years, and also the seven empty ears blasted with the east wind; they shall be seven years of famine.

searchText: genesis 41:28 that is the thing which i spoke unto pharaoh: what god is about to do he hath shown unto pharaoh.
english: That is the thing which I spoke unto Pharaoh: what God is about to do He hath shown unto Pharaoh.

searchText: genesis 41:29 behold, there come seven years of great plenty throughout all the land of egypt.
english: Behold, there come seven years of great plenty throughout all the land of Egypt.

searchText: genesis 41:30 and there shall arise after them seven years of famine; and all the plenty shall be forgotten in the land of egypt; and the famine shall consume the land;
english: And there shall arise after them seven years of famine; and all the plenty shall be forgotten in the land of Egypt; and the famine shall consume the land;

searchText: genesis 41:31 and the plenty shall not be known in the land by reason of that famine which followeth; for it shall be very grievous.
english: and the plenty shall not be known in the land by reason of that famine which followeth; for it shall be very grievous.

searchText: genesis 41:32 and for that the dream was doubled unto pharaoh twice, it is because the thing is established by god, and god will shortly bring it to pass.
english: And for that the dream was doubled unto Pharaoh twice, it is because the thing is established by God, and God will shortly bring it to pass.

searchText: genesis 41:33 now therefore let pharaoh look out a man discreet and wise, and set him over the land of egypt.
english: Now therefore let Pharaoh look out a man discreet and wise, and set him over the land of Egypt.

searchText: genesis 41:34 let pharaoh do this, and let him appoint overseers over the land, and take up the fifth part of the land of egypt in the seven years of plenty.
english: Let Pharaoh do this, and let him appoint overseers over the land, and take up the fifth part of the land of Egypt in the seven years of plenty.

searchText: genesis 41:35 and let them gather all the food of these good years that come, and lay up corn under the hand of pharaoh for food in the cities, and let them keep it.
english: And let them gather all the food of these good years that come, and lay up corn under the hand of Pharaoh for food in the cities, and let them keep it.

searchText: genesis 41:36 and the food shall be for a store to the land against the seven years of famine, which shall be in the land of egypt; that the land perish not through the famine.’
english: And the food shall be for a store to the land against the seven years of famine, which shall be in the land of Egypt; that the land perish not through the famine.’

searchText: genesis 41:37 and the thing was good in the eyes of pharaoh, and in the eyes of all his servants.
english: And the thing was good in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of all his servants.

searchText: genesis 41:38 and pharaoh said unto his servants: ‘can we find such a one as this, a man in whom the spirit of god is?’
english: And Pharaoh said unto his servants: ‘Can we find such a one as this, a man in whom the spirit of God is?’

searchText: genesis 41:39 and pharaoh said unto joseph: ‘forasmuch as god hath shown thee all this, there is none so discreet and wise as thou.
english: And Pharaoh said unto Joseph: ‘Forasmuch as God hath shown thee all this, there is none so discreet and wise as thou.

searchText: genesis 41:40 thou shalt be over my house, and according unto thy word shall all my people be ruled; only in the throne will i be greater than thou.’
english: Thou shalt be over my house, and according unto thy word shall all my people be ruled; only in the throne will I be greater than thou.’

searchText: genesis 41:41 and pharaoh said unto joseph: ‘see, i have set thee over all the land of egypt.’
english: And Pharaoh said unto Joseph: ‘See, I have set thee over all the land of Egypt.’

searchText: genesis 41:42 and pharaoh took off his signet ring from his hand, and put it upon joseph’s hand, and arrayed him in vestures of fine linen, and put a gold chain about his neck.
english: And Pharaoh took off his signet ring from his hand, and put it upon Joseph’s hand, and arrayed him in vestures of fine linen, and put a gold chain about his neck.

searchText: genesis 41:43 and he made him to ride in the second chariot which he had; and they cried before him: ‘abrech’; and he set him over all the land of egypt.
english: And he made him to ride in the second chariot which he had; and they cried before him: ‘Abrech’; and he set him over all the land of Egypt.

searchText: genesis 41:44 and pharaoh said unto joseph: ‘i am pharaoh, and without thee shall no man lift up his hand or his foot in all the land of egypt.’
english: And Pharaoh said unto Joseph: ‘I am Pharaoh, and without thee shall no man lift up his hand or his foot in all the land of Egypt.’

searchText: genesis 41:45 and pharaoh called joseph’s name zaphenath-paneah; and he gave him to wife asenath the daughter of poti-phera priest of on. and joseph went out over the land of egypt.—
english: And Pharaoh called Joseph’s name Zaphenath-paneah; and he gave him to wife Asenath the daughter of Poti-phera priest of On. And Joseph went out over the land of Egypt.—

searchText: genesis 41:46 and joseph was thirty years old when he stood before pharaoh king of egypt.—and joseph went out from the presence of pharaoh, and went throughout all the land of egypt.
english: And Joseph was thirty years old when he stood before Pharaoh king of Egypt.—And Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh, and went throughout all the land of Egypt.

searchText: genesis 41:47 and in the seven years of plenty the earth brought forth in heaps.
english: And in the seven years of plenty the earth brought forth in heaps.

searchText: genesis 41:48 and he gathered up all the food of the seven years which were in the land of egypt, and laid up the food in the cities; the food of the field, which was round about every city, laid he up in the same.
english: And he gathered up all the food of the seven years which were in the land of Egypt, and laid up the food in the cities; the food of the field, which was round about every city, laid he up in the same.

searchText: genesis 41:49 and joseph laid up corn as the sand of the sea, very much, until they left off numbering; for it was without number.
english: And Joseph laid up corn as the sand of the sea, very much, until they left off numbering; for it was without number.

searchText: genesis 41:50 and unto joseph were born two sons before the year of famine came, whom asenath the daughter of poti-phera priest of on bore unto him.
english: And unto Joseph were born two sons before the year of famine came, whom Asenath the daughter of Poti-phera priest of On bore unto him.

searchText: genesis 41:51 and joseph called the name of the first-born manasseh: ‘for god hath made me forget all my toil, and all my father’s house.’
english: And Joseph called the name of the first-born Manasseh: ‘for God hath made me forget all my toil, and all my father’s house.’

searchText: genesis 41:52 and the name of the second called he ephraim: ‘for god hath made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.’
english: And the name of the second called he Ephraim: ‘for God hath made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.’

searchText: genesis 41:53 and the seven years of plenty, that was in the land of egypt, came to an end.
english: And the seven years of plenty, that was in the land of Egypt, came to an end.

searchText: genesis 41:54 and the seven years of famine began to come, according as joseph had said; and there was famine in all lands; but in all the land of egypt there was bread.
english: And the seven years of famine began to come, according as Joseph had said; and there was famine in all lands; but in all the land of Egypt there was bread.

searchText: genesis 41:55 and when all the land of egypt was famished, the people cried to pharaoh for bread; and pharaoh said unto all the egyptians: ‘go unto joseph; what he saith to you, do.’
english: And when all the land of Egypt was famished, the people cried to Pharaoh for bread; and Pharaoh said unto all the Egyptians: ‘Go unto Joseph; what he saith to you, do.’

searchText: genesis 41:56 and the famine was over all the face of the earth; and joseph opened all the storehouses, and sold unto the egyptians; and the famine was sore in the land of egypt.
english: And the famine was over all the face of the earth; and Joseph opened all the storehouses, and sold unto the Egyptians; and the famine was sore in the land of Egypt.

searchText: genesis 41:57 and all countries came into egypt to joseph to buy corn; because the famine was sore in all the earth.
english: And all countries came into Egypt to Joseph to buy corn; because the famine was sore in all the earth.

searchText: genesis 42:1 now jacob saw that there was corn in egypt, and jacob said unto his sons: ‘why do ye look one upon another?’
english: Now Jacob saw that there was corn in Egypt, and Jacob said unto his sons: ‘Why do ye look one upon another?’

searchText: genesis 42:2 and he said: ‘behold, i have heard that there is corn in egypt. get you down thither, and buy for us from thence; that we may live, and not die.’
english: And he said: ‘Behold, I have heard that there is corn in Egypt. Get you down thither, and buy for us from thence; that we may live, and not die.’

searchText: genesis 42:3 and joseph’s ten brethren went down to buy corn from egypt.
english: And Joseph’s ten brethren went down to buy corn from Egypt.

searchText: genesis 42:4 but benjamin, joseph’s brother, jacob sent not with his brethren; for he said: ‘lest peradventure harm befall him.’
english: But Benjamin, Joseph’s brother, Jacob sent not with his brethren; for he said: ‘Lest peradventure harm befall him.’

searchText: genesis 42:5 and the sons of israel came to buy among those that came; for the famine was in the land of caanan.
english: And the sons of Israel came to buy among those that came; for the famine was in the land of Caanan.

searchText: genesis 42:6 and joseph was the governor over the land; he it was that sold to all the people of the land. and joseph’s brethren came, and bowed down to him with their faces to the earth.
english: And Joseph was the governor over the land; he it was that sold to all the people of the land. And Joseph’s brethren came, and bowed down to him with their faces to the earth.

searchText: genesis 42:7 and joseph saw his brethren, and he knew them, but made himself strange unto them, and spoke roughly with them; and he said unto them: ‘whence come ye?’ and they said: ‘from the land of canaan to buy food.’
english: And Joseph saw his brethren, and he knew them, but made himself strange unto them, and spoke roughly with them; and he said unto them: ‘Whence come ye?’ And they said: ‘From the land of Canaan to buy food.’

searchText: genesis 42:8 and joseph knew his brethren, but they knew him not.
english: And Joseph knew his brethren, but they knew him not.

searchText: genesis 42:9 and joseph remembered the dreams which he dreamed of them, and said unto them: ‘ye are spies; to see the nakedness of the land ye are come.’
english: And Joseph remembered the dreams which he dreamed of them, and said unto them: ‘Ye are spies; to see the nakedness of the land ye are come.’

searchText: genesis 42:10 and they said unto him: ‘nay, my lord, but to buy food are thy servants come.
english: And they said unto him: ‘Nay, my lord, but to buy food are thy servants come.

searchText: genesis 42:11 we are all one man’s sons; we are upright men, thy servants are no spies.’
english: We are all one man’s sons; we are upright men, thy servants are no spies.’

searchText: genesis 42:12 and he said unto them: ‘nay, but to see the nakedness of the land ye are come.’
english: And he said unto them: ‘Nay, but to see the nakedness of the land ye are come.’

searchText: genesis 42:13 and they said: ‘we thy servants are twelve brethren, the sons of one man in the land of canaan; and, behold, the youngest is this day with our father, and one is not.’
english: And they said: ‘We thy servants are twelve brethren, the sons of one man in the land of Canaan; and, behold, the youngest is this day with our father, and one is not.’

searchText: genesis 42:14 and joseph said unto them: ‘that is it that i spoke unto you, saying: ye are spies.
english: And Joseph said unto them: ‘That is it that I spoke unto you, saying: Ye are spies.

searchText: genesis 42:15 hereby ye shall be proved, as pharaoh liveth, ye shall not go forth hence, except your youngest brother come hither.
english: Hereby ye shall be proved, as Pharaoh liveth, ye shall not go forth hence, except your youngest brother come hither.

searchText: genesis 42:16 send one of you, and let him fetch your brother, and ye shall be bound, that your words may be proved, whether there be truth in you; or else, as pharaoh liveth, surely ye are spies.’
english: Send one of you, and let him fetch your brother, and ye shall be bound, that your words may be proved, whether there be truth in you; or else, as Pharaoh liveth, surely ye are spies.’

searchText: genesis 42:17 and he put them all together into ward three days.
english: And he put them all together into ward three days.

searchText: genesis 42:18 and joseph said unto them the third day.’ this do, and live; for i fear god:
english: And Joseph said unto them the third day.’ This do, and live; for I fear God:

searchText: genesis 42:19 if ye be upright men, let one of your brethren be bound in your prison-house; but go ye, carry corn for the famine of your houses;
english: if ye be upright men, let one of your brethren be bound in your prison-house; but go ye, carry corn for the famine of your houses;

searchText: genesis 42:20 and bring your youngest brother unto me; so shall your words be verified, and ye shall not die.’ and they did so.
english: and bring your youngest brother unto me; so shall your words be verified, and ye shall not die.’ And they did so.

searchText: genesis 42:21 and they said one to another: ‘we are verily guilty concerning our brother, in that we saw the distress of his soul, when he besought us, and we would not hear; therefore is this distress come upon us.’
english: And they said one to another: ‘We are verily guilty concerning our brother, in that we saw the distress of his soul, when he besought us, and we would not hear; therefore is this distress come upon us.’

searchText: genesis 42:22 and reuben answered them, saying: ‘spoke i not unto you, saying: do not sin against the child; and ye would not hear? therefore also, behold, his blood is required.’
english: And Reuben answered them, saying: ‘Spoke I not unto you, saying: Do not sin against the child; and ye would not hear? therefore also, behold, his blood is required.’

searchText: genesis 42:23 and they knew not that joseph understood them; for the interpreter was between them.
english: And they knew not that Joseph understood them; for the interpreter was between them.

searchText: genesis 42:24 and he turned himself about from them, and wept; and he returned to them, and spoke to them, and took simeon from among them, and bound him before their eyes.
english: And he turned himself about from them, and wept; and he returned to them, and spoke to them, and took Simeon from among them, and bound him before their eyes.

searchText: genesis 42:25 then joseph commanded to fill their vessels with corn, and to restore every man’s money into his sack, and to give them provision for the way; and thus was it done unto them.
english: Then Joseph commanded to fill their vessels with corn, and to restore every man’s money into his sack, and to give them provision for the way; and thus was it done unto them.

searchText: genesis 42:26 and they laded their asses with their corn, and departed thence.
english: And they laded their asses with their corn, and departed thence.

searchText: genesis 42:27 and as one of them opened his sack to give his ass provender in the lodging-place, he espied his money; and, behold, it was in the mouth of his sack.
english: And as one of them opened his sack to give his ass provender in the lodging-place, he espied his money; and, behold, it was in the mouth of his sack.

searchText: genesis 42:28 and he said unto his brethren: ‘my money is restored; and, lo, it is even in my sack.’ and their heart failed them, and they turned trembling one to another, saying: ‘what is this that god hath done unto us?’
english: And he said unto his brethren: ‘My money is restored; and, lo, it is even in my sack.’ And their heart failed them, and they turned trembling one to another, saying: ‘What is this that God hath done unto us?’

searchText: genesis 42:29 and they came unto jacob their father unto the land of canaan, and told him all that had befallen them, saying:
english: And they came unto Jacob their father unto the land of Canaan, and told him all that had befallen them, saying:

searchText: genesis 42:30 ’the man, the lord of the land, spoke roughly with us, and took us for spies of the country.
english: ’The man, the lord of the land, spoke roughly with us, and took us for spies of the country.

searchText: genesis 42:31 and we said unto him: we are upright men; we are no spies.
english: And we said unto him: We are upright men; we are no spies.

searchText: genesis 42:32 we are twelve brethren, sons of our father; one is not, and the youngest is this day with our father in the land of canaan. .
english: We are twelve brethren, sons of our father; one is not, and the youngest is this day with our father in the land of Canaan. .

searchText: genesis 42:33 and the man, the lord of the land, said unto us: hereby shall i know that ye are upright men: leave one of your brethren with me, and take corn for the famine of your houses, and go your way.
english: And the man, the lord of the land, said unto us: Hereby shall I know that ye are upright men: leave one of your brethren with me, and take corn for the famine of your houses, and go your way.

searchText: genesis 42:34 and bring your youngest brother unto me; then shall i know that ye are no spies, but that ye are upright men; so will i deliver you your brother, and ye shall traffic in the land.’
english: And bring your youngest brother unto me; then shall I know that ye are no spies, but that ye are upright men; so will I deliver you your brother, and ye shall traffic in the land.’

searchText: genesis 42:35 and it came to pass as they emptied their sacks, that, behold, every man’s bundle of money was in his sack; and when they and their father saw their bundles of money, they were afraid.
english: And it came to pass as they emptied their sacks, that, behold, every man’s bundle of money was in his sack; and when they and their father saw their bundles of money, they were afraid.

searchText: genesis 42:36 and jacob their father said unto them: ‘me have ye bereaved of my children: joseph is not, and simeon is not, and ye will take benjamin away; upon me are all these things come.’
english: And Jacob their father said unto them: ‘Me have ye bereaved of my children: Joseph is not, and Simeon is not, and ye will take Benjamin away; upon me are all these things come.’

searchText: genesis 42:37 and reuben spoke unto his father, saying: ‘thou shalt slay my two sons, if i bring him not to thee; deliver him into my hand, and i will bring him back to thee.’
english: And Reuben spoke unto his father, saying: ‘Thou shalt slay my two sons, if I bring him not to thee; deliver him into my hand, and I will bring him back to thee.’

searchText: genesis 42:38 and he said: ‘my son shall not go down with you; for his brother is dead, and he only is left; if harm befall him by the way in which ye go, then will ye bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to the grave.
english: And he said: ‘My son shall not go down with you; for his brother is dead, and he only is left; if harm befall him by the way in which ye go, then will ye bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to the grave.

searchText: genesis 43:1 and the famine was sore in the land.
english: And the famine was sore in the land.

searchText: genesis 43:2 and it came to pass, when they had eaten up the corn which they had brought out of egypt, that their father said unto them: ‘go again, buy us a little food.’
english: And it came to pass, when they had eaten up the corn which they had brought out of Egypt, that their father said unto them: ‘Go again, buy us a little food.’

searchText: genesis 43:3 and judah spoke unto him, saying: ‘the man did earnestly forewarn us, saying: ye shall not see my face, except your brother be with you.
english: And Judah spoke unto him, saying: ‘The man did earnestly forewarn us, saying: Ye shall not see my face, except your brother be with you.

searchText: genesis 43:4 if thou wilt send our brother with us, we will go down and buy thee food;
english: If thou wilt send our brother with us, we will go down and buy thee food;

searchText: genesis 43:5 but if thou wilt not send him, we will not go down, for the man said unto us: ye shall not see my face, except your brother be with you.’
english: but if thou wilt not send him, we will not go down, for the man said unto us: Ye shall not see my face, except your brother be with you.’

searchText: genesis 43:6 and israel said: ‘wherefore dealt ye so ill with me, as to tell the man whether ye had yet a brother?’
english: And Israel said: ‘Wherefore dealt ye so ill with me, as to tell the man whether ye had yet a brother?’

searchText: genesis 43:7 and they said: ‘the man asked straitly concerning ourselves, and concerning our kindred, saying: is your father yet alive? have ye another brother? and we told him according to the tenor of these words; could we in any wise know that he would say: bring your brother down?’
english: And they said: ‘The man asked straitly concerning ourselves, and concerning our kindred, saying: Is your father yet alive? have ye another brother? and we told him according to the tenor of these words; could we in any wise know that he would say: Bring your brother down?’

searchText: genesis 43:8 and judah said unto israel his father: ‘send the lad with me, and we will arise and go, that we may live, and not die, both we, and thou, and also our little ones.
english: And Judah said unto Israel his father: ‘Send the lad with me, and we will arise and go, that we may live, and not die, both we, and thou, and also our little ones.

searchText: genesis 43:9 i will be surety for him; of my hand shalt thou require him; if i bring him not unto thee, and set him before thee, then let me bear the blame for ever.
english: I will be surety for him; of my hand shalt thou require him; if I bring him not unto thee, and set him before thee, then let me bear the blame for ever.

searchText: genesis 43:10 for except we had lingered, surely we had now returned a second time.’
english: For except we had lingered, surely we had now returned a second time.’

searchText: genesis 43:11 and their father israel said unto them: ‘if it be so now, do this: take of the choice fruits of the land in your vessels, and carry down the man a present, a little balm, and a little honey, spicery and ladanum, nuts, and almonds;
english: And their father Israel said unto them: ‘If it be so now, do this: take of the choice fruits of the land in your vessels, and carry down the man a present, a little balm, and a little honey, spicery and ladanum, nuts, and almonds;

searchText: genesis 43:12 and take double money in your hand; and the money that was returned in the mouth of your sacks carry back in your hand; peradventure it was an oversight;
english: and take double money in your hand; and the money that was returned in the mouth of your sacks carry back in your hand; peradventure it was an oversight;

searchText: genesis 43:13 take also your brother, and arise, go again unto the man;
english: take also your brother, and arise, go again unto the man;

searchText: genesis 43:14 and god almighty give you mercy before the man, that he may release unto you your other brother and benjamin. and as for me, if i be bereaved of my children, i am bereaved.’
english: and God Almighty give you mercy before the man, that he may release unto you your other brother and Benjamin. And as for me, if I be bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.’

searchText: genesis 43:15 and the men took that present, and they took double money in their hand, and benjamin; and rose up, and went down to egypt, and stood before joseph.
english: And the men took that present, and they took double money in their hand, and Benjamin; and rose up, and went down to Egypt, and stood before Joseph.

searchText: genesis 43:16 and when joseph saw benjamin with them, he said to the steward of his house: ‘bring the men into the house, and kill the beasts, and prepare the meat; for the men shall dine with me at noon.’
english: And when Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the steward of his house: ‘Bring the men into the house, and kill the beasts, and prepare the meat; for the men shall dine with me at noon.’

searchText: genesis 43:17 and the man did as joseph bade; and the man brought the men into joseph’s house.
english: And the man did as Joseph bade; and the man brought the men into Joseph’s house.

searchText: genesis 43:18 and the men were afraid, because they were brought into joseph’s house; and they said: ‘because of the money that was returned in our sacks at the first time are we brought in; that he may seek occasion against us, and fall upon us, and take us for bondmen, and our asses.’
english: And the men were afraid, because they were brought into Joseph’s house; and they said: ‘Because of the money that was returned in our sacks at the first time are we brought in; that he may seek occasion against us, and fall upon us, and take us for bondmen, and our asses.’

searchText: genesis 43:19 and they came near to the steward of joseph’s house, and they spoke unto him at the door of the house,
english: And they came near to the steward of Joseph’s house, and they spoke unto him at the door of the house,

searchText: genesis 43:20 and said: ‘oh my lord, we came indeed down at the first time to buy food.
english: and said: ‘Oh my lord, we came indeed down at the first time to buy food.

searchText: genesis 43:21 and it came to pass, when we came to the lodging-place, that we opened our sacks, and, behold, every man’s money was in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight; and we have brought it back in our hand.
english: And it came to pass, when we came to the lodging-place, that we opened our sacks, and, behold, every man’s money was in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight; and we have brought it back in our hand.

searchText: genesis 43:22 and other money have we brought down in our hand to buy food. we know not who put our money in our sacks.’
english: And other money have we brought down in our hand to buy food. We know not who put our money in our sacks.’

searchText: genesis 43:23 and he said: ‘peace be to you, fear not; your god, and the god of your father, hath given you treasure in your sacks; i had your money.’ and he brought simeon out unto them.
english: And he said: ‘Peace be to you, fear not; your God, and the God of your father, hath given you treasure in your sacks; I had your money.’ And he brought Simeon out unto them.

searchText: genesis 43:24 and the man brought the men into joseph’s house, and gave them water, and they washed their feet; and he gave their asses provender.
english: And the man brought the men into Joseph’s house, and gave them water, and they washed their feet; and he gave their asses provender.

searchText: genesis 43:25 and they made ready the present against joseph’s coming at noon; for they heard that they should eat bread there.
english: And they made ready the present against Joseph’s coming at noon; for they heard that they should eat bread there.

searchText: genesis 43:26 and when joseph came home, they brought him the present which was in their hand into the house, and bowed down to him to the earth.
english: And when Joseph came home, they brought him the present which was in their hand into the house, and bowed down to him to the earth.

searchText: genesis 43:27 and he asked them of their welfare, and said: ‘is your father well, the old man of whom ye spoke? is he yet alive?’
english: And he asked them of their welfare, and said: ‘Is your father well, the old man of whom ye spoke? Is he yet alive?’

searchText: genesis 43:28 and they said: ‘thy servant our father is well, he is yet alive.’ and they bowed the head, and made obeisance.
english: And they said: ‘Thy servant our father is well, he is yet alive.’ And they bowed the head, and made obeisance.

searchText: genesis 43:29 and he lifted up his eyes, and saw benjamin his brother, his mother’s son, and said: ‘is this your youngest brother of whom ye spoke unto me?’ and he said: ‘god be gracious unto thee, my son.’
english: And he lifted up his eyes, and saw Benjamin his brother, his mother’s son, and said: ‘Is this your youngest brother of whom ye spoke unto me?’ And he said: ‘God be gracious unto thee, my son.’

searchText: genesis 43:30 and joseph made haste; for his heart yearned toward his brother; and he sought where to weep; and he entered into his chamber, and wept there.
english: And Joseph made haste; for his heart yearned toward his brother; and he sought where to weep; and he entered into his chamber, and wept there.

searchText: genesis 43:31 and he washed his face, and came out; and he refrained himself, and said: ‘set on bread.’
english: And he washed his face, and came out; and he refrained himself, and said: ‘Set on bread.’

searchText: genesis 43:32 and they set on for him by himself, and for them by themselves, and for the egyptians, that did eat with him, by themselves; because the egyptians might not eat bread with the hebrews; for that is an abomination unto the egyptians.
english: And they set on for him by himself, and for them by themselves, and for the Egyptians, that did eat with him, by themselves; because the Egyptians might not eat bread with the Hebrews; for that is an abomination unto the Egyptians.

searchText: genesis 43:33 and they sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright, and the youngest according to his youth; and the men marvelled one with another.
english: And they sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright, and the youngest according to his youth; and the men marvelled one with another.

searchText: genesis 43:34 and portions were taken unto them from before him; but benjamin’s portion was five times so much as any of theirs. and they drank, and were merry with him.
english: And portions were taken unto them from before him; but Benjamin’s portion was five times so much as any of theirs. And they drank, and were merry with him.

searchText: genesis 44:1 and he commanded the steward of his house, saying: ‘fill the men’s sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put every man’s money in his sack’s mouth.
english: And he commanded the steward of his house, saying: ‘Fill the men’s sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put every man’s money in his sack’s mouth.

searchText: genesis 44:2 and put my goblet, the silver goblet, in the sack’s mouth of the youngest, and his corn money.’ and he did according to the word that joseph had spoken.
english: And put my goblet, the silver goblet, in the sack’s mouth of the youngest, and his corn money.’ And he did according to the word that Joseph had spoken.

searchText: genesis 44:3 as soon as the morning was light, the men were sent away, they and their asses.
english: As soon as the morning was light, the men were sent away, they and their asses.

searchText: genesis 44:4 and when they were gone out of the city, and were not yet far off, joseph said unto his steward: ‘up, follow after the men; and when thou dost overtake them, say unto them: wherefore have ye rewarded evil for good?
english: And when they were gone out of the city, and were not yet far off, Joseph said unto his steward: ‘Up, follow after the men; and when thou dost overtake them, say unto them: Wherefore have ye rewarded evil for good?

searchText: genesis 44:5 is not this it in which my lord drinketh, and whereby he indeed divineth? ye have done evil in so doing.’
english: Is not this it in which my lord drinketh, and whereby he indeed divineth? ye have done evil in so doing.’

searchText: genesis 44:6 and he overtook them, and he spoke unto them these words.
english: And he overtook them, and he spoke unto them these words.

searchText: genesis 44:7 and they said unto him: ‘wherefore speaketh my lord such words as these? far be it from thy servants that they should do such a thing.
english: And they said unto him: ‘Wherefore speaketh my lord such words as these? Far be it from thy servants that they should do such a thing.

searchText: genesis 44:8 behold, the money, which we found in our sacks’mouths, we brought back unto thee out of the land of canaan; how then should we steal out of thy lord’s house silver or gold?
english: Behold, the money, which we found in our sacks’mouths, we brought back unto thee out of the land of Canaan; how then should we steal out of thy lord’s house silver or gold?

searchText: genesis 44:9 with whomsoever of thy servants it be found, let him die, and we also will be my lord’s bondmen.’
english: With whomsoever of thy servants it be found, let him die, and we also will be my lord’s bondmen.’

searchText: genesis 44:10 and he said: ‘now also let it be according unto your words: he with whom it is found shall be my bondman; and ye shall be blameless.’
english: And he said: ‘Now also let it be according unto your words: he with whom it is found shall be my bondman; and ye shall be blameless.’

searchText: genesis 44:11 then they hastened, and took down every man his sack to the ground, and opened every man his sack.
english: Then they hastened, and took down every man his sack to the ground, and opened every man his sack.

searchText: genesis 44:12 and he searched, beginning at the eldest, and leaving off at the youngest; and the goblet was found in benjamin’s sack.
english: And he searched, beginning at the eldest, and leaving off at the youngest; and the goblet was found in Benjamin’s sack.

searchText: genesis 44:13 and they rent their clothes, and laded every man his ass, and returned to the city.
english: And they rent their clothes, and laded every man his ass, and returned to the city.

searchText: genesis 44:14 and judah and his brethren came to joseph’s house, and he was yet there; and they fell before him on the ground.
english: And Judah and his brethren came to Joseph’s house, and he was yet there; and they fell before him on the ground.

searchText: genesis 44:15 and joseph said unto them: ‘what deed is this that ye have done? know ye not that such a man as i will indeed divine?’
english: And Joseph said unto them: ‘What deed is this that ye have done? know ye not that such a man as I will indeed divine?’

searchText: genesis 44:16 and judah said: ‘what shall we say unto my lord? what shall we speak? or how shall we clear ourselves? god hath found out the iniquity of thy servants; behold, we are my lord’s bondmen, both we, and he also in whose hand the cup is found.’
english: And Judah said: ‘What shall we say unto my lord? what shall we speak? or how shall we clear ourselves? God hath found out the iniquity of thy servants; behold, we are my lord’s bondmen, both we, and he also in whose hand the cup is found.’

searchText: genesis 44:17 and he said: ‘far be it from me that i should do so; the man in whose hand the goblet is found, he shall be my bondman; but as for you, get you up in peace unto your father.’
english: And he said: ‘Far be it from me that I should do so; the man in whose hand the goblet is found, he shall be my bondman; but as for you, get you up in peace unto your father.’

searchText: genesis 44:18 then judah came near unto him, and said: ‘oh my lord, let thy servant, i pray thee, speak a word in my lord’s ears, and let not thine anger burn against thy servant; for thou art even as pharaoh.
english: Then Judah came near unto him, and said: ‘Oh my lord, let thy servant, I pray thee, speak a word in my lord’s ears, and let not thine anger burn against thy servant; for thou art even as Pharaoh.

searchText: genesis 44:19 my lord asked his servants, saying: have ye a father, or a brother?
english: My lord asked his servants, saying: Have ye a father, or a brother?

searchText: genesis 44:20 and we said unto my lord: we have a father, an old man, and a child of his old age, a little one; and his brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother, and his father loveth him.
english: And we said unto my lord: We have a father, an old man, and a child of his old age, a little one; and his brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother, and his father loveth him.

searchText: genesis 44:21 and thou saidst unto thy servants: bring him down unto me, that i may set mine eyes upon him.
english: And thou saidst unto thy servants: Bring him down unto me, that I may set mine eyes upon him.

searchText: genesis 44:22 and we said unto my lord: the lad cannot leave his father; for if he should leave his father, his father would die.
english: And we said unto my lord: The lad cannot leave his father; for if he should leave his father, his father would die.

searchText: genesis 44:23 and thou saidst unto thy servants: except your youngest brother come down with you, ye shall see my face no more.
english: And thou saidst unto thy servants: Except your youngest brother come down with you, ye shall see my face no more.

searchText: genesis 44:24 and it came to pass when we came up unto thy servant my father, we told him the words of my lord.
english: And it came to pass when we came up unto thy servant my father, we told him the words of my lord.

searchText: genesis 44:25 and our father said: go again, buy us a little food.
english: And our father said: Go again, buy us a little food.

searchText: genesis 44:26 and we said: we cannot go down; if our youngest brother be with us, then will we go down; for we may not see the man’s face, except our youngest brother be with us.
english: And we said: We cannot go down; if our youngest brother be with us, then will we go down; for we may not see the man’s face, except our youngest brother be with us.

searchText: genesis 44:27 and thy servant my father said unto us: ye know that my wife bore me two sons;
english: And thy servant my father said unto us: Ye know that my wife bore me two sons;

searchText: genesis 44:28 and the one went out from me, and i said: surely he is torn in pieces; and i have not seen him since;
english: and the one went out from me, and I said: Surely he is torn in pieces; and I have not seen him since;

searchText: genesis 44:29 and if ye take this one also from me, and harm befall him, ye will bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to the grave.
english: and if ye take this one also from me, and harm befall him, ye will bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to the grave.

searchText: genesis 44:30 now therefore when i come to thy servant my father, and the lad is not with us; seeing that his soul is bound up with the lad’s soul;
english: Now therefore when I come to thy servant my father, and the lad is not with us; seeing that his soul is bound up with the lad’s soul;

searchText: genesis 44:31 it will come to pass, when he seeth that the lad is not with us, that he will die; and thy servants will bring down the gray hairs of thy servant our father with sorrow to the grave.
english: it will come to pass, when he seeth that the lad is not with us, that he will die; and thy servants will bring down the gray hairs of thy servant our father with sorrow to the grave.

searchText: genesis 44:32 for thy servant became surety for the lad unto my father, saying: if i bring him not unto thee, then shall i bear the blame to my father for ever.
english: For thy servant became surety for the lad unto my father, saying: If I bring him not unto thee, then shall I bear the blame to my father for ever.

searchText: genesis 44:33 now therefore, let thy servant, i pray thee, abide instead of the lad a bondman to my lord; and let the lad go up with his brethren.
english: Now therefore, let thy servant, I pray thee, abide instead of the lad a bondman to my lord; and let the lad go up with his brethren.

searchText: genesis 44:34 for how shall i go up to my father, if the lad be not with me? lest i look upon the evil that shall come on my father.’
english: For how shall I go up to my father, if the lad be not with me? lest I look upon the evil that shall come on my father.’

searchText: genesis 45:1 then joseph could not refrain himself before all them that stood by him; and he cried: ‘cause every man to go out from me.’ and there stood no man with him, while joseph made himself known unto his brethren.
english: Then Joseph could not refrain himself before all them that stood by him; and he cried: ‘Cause every man to go out from me.’ And there stood no man with him, while Joseph made himself known unto his brethren.

searchText: genesis 45:2 and he wept aloud; and the egyptians heard, and the house of pharaoh heard.
english: And he wept aloud; and the Egyptians heard, and the house of Pharaoh heard.

searchText: genesis 45:3 and joseph said unto his brethren: ‘i am joseph; doth my father yet live?’ and his brethren could not answer him; for they were affrighted at his presence.
english: And Joseph said unto his brethren: ‘I am Joseph; doth my father yet live?’ And his brethren could not answer him; for they were affrighted at his presence.

searchText: genesis 45:4 and joseph said unto his brethren: ‘come near to me, i pray you.’ and they came near. and he said: ‘i am joseph your brother, whom ye sold into egypt.
english: And Joseph said unto his brethren: ‘Come near to me, I pray you.’ And they came near. And he said: ‘I am Joseph your brother, whom ye sold into Egypt.

searchText: genesis 45:5 and now be not grieved, nor angry with yourselves, that ye sold me hither; for god did send me before you to preserve life.
english: And now be not grieved, nor angry with yourselves, that ye sold me hither; for God did send me before you to preserve life.

searchText: genesis 45:6 for these two years hath the famine been in the land; and there are yet five years, in which there shall be neither plowing nor harvest.
english: For these two years hath the famine been in the land; and there are yet five years, in which there shall be neither plowing nor harvest.

searchText: genesis 45:7 and god sent me before you to give you a remnant on the earth, and to save you alive for a great deliverance.
english: And God sent me before you to give you a remnant on the earth, and to save you alive for a great deliverance.

searchText: genesis 45:8 so now it was not you that sent me hither, but god; and he hath made me a father to pharaoh, and lord of all his house, and ruler over all the land of egypt.
english: So now it was not you that sent me hither, but God; and He hath made me a father to Pharaoh, and lord of all his house, and ruler over all the land of Egypt.
