searchText: exodus 4:20 and moses took his wife and his sons, and set them upon an ass, and he returned to the land of egypt; and moses took the rod of god in his hand.
english: And Moses took his wife and his sons, and set them upon an ass, and he returned to the land of Egypt; and Moses took the rod of God in his hand.

searchText: exodus 4:21 and the lord said unto moses: ‘when thou goest back into egypt, see that thou do before pharaoh all the wonders which i have put in thy hand; but i will harden his heart, and he will not let the people go.
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘When thou goest back into Egypt, see that thou do before Pharaoh all the wonders which I have put in thy hand; but I will harden his heart, and he will not let the people go.

searchText: exodus 4:22 and thou shalt say unto pharaoh: thus saith the lord: israel is my son, my first-born.
english: And thou shalt say unto Pharaoh: Thus saith the LORD: Israel is My son, My first-born.

searchText: exodus 4:23 and i have said unto thee: let my son go, that he may serve me; and thou hast refused to let him go. ‘behold, i will slay thy first-born.’
english: And I have said unto thee: Let My son go, that he may serve Me; and thou hast refused to let him go. ‘Behold, I will slay thy first-born.’

searchText: exodus 4:24 and it came to pass on the way at the lodging-place, that the lord met him, and sought to kill him.
english: And it came to pass on the way at the lodging-place, that the LORD met him, and sought to kill him.

searchText: exodus 4:25 then zipporah took a flint, and cut off the foreskin of her son, and cast it at his feet; and she said: ‘surely a bridegroom of blood art thou to me.’
english: Then Zipporah took a flint, and cut off the foreskin of her son, and cast it at his feet; and she said: ‘Surely a bridegroom of blood art thou to me.’

searchText: exodus 4:26 so he let him alone. then she said: ‘a bridegroom of blood in regard of the circumcision.’
english: So He let him alone. Then she said: ‘A bridegroom of blood in regard of the circumcision.’

searchText: exodus 4:27 and the lord said to aaron: ‘go into the wilderness to meet moses.’ and he went, and met him in the mountain of god, and kissed him.
english: And the LORD said to Aaron: ‘Go into the wilderness to meet Moses.’ And he went, and met him in the mountain of God, and kissed him.

searchText: exodus 4:28 and moses told aaron all the words of the lord wherewith he had sent him, and all the signs wherewith he had charged him.
english: And Moses told Aaron all the words of the LORD wherewith He had sent him, and all the signs wherewith He had charged him.

searchText: exodus 4:29 and moses and aaron went and gathered together all the elders of the children of israel.
english: And Moses and Aaron went and gathered together all the elders of the children of Israel.

searchText: exodus 4:30 and aaron spoke all the words which the lord had spoken unto moses, and did the signs in the sight of the people.
english: And Aaron spoke all the words which the LORD had spoken unto Moses, and did the signs in the sight of the people.

searchText: exodus 4:31 and the people believed; and when they heard that the lord had remembered the children of israel, and that he had seen their affliction, then they bowed their heads and worshipped.
english: And the people believed; and when they heard that the LORD had remembered the children of Israel, and that He had seen their affliction, then they bowed their heads and worshipped.

searchText: exodus 5:1 and afterward moses and aaron came, and said unto pharaoh: ‘thus saith the lord, the god of israel: let my people go, that they may hold a feast unto me in the wilderness.’
english: And afterward Moses and Aaron came, and said unto Pharaoh: ‘Thus saith the LORD, the God of Israel: Let My people go, that they may hold a feast unto Me in the wilderness.’

searchText: exodus 5:2 and pharaoh said: ‘who is the lord, that i should hearken unto his voice to let israel go? i know not the lord, and moreover i will not let israel go.’
english: And Pharaoh said: ‘Who is the LORD, that I should hearken unto His voice to let Israel go? I know not the LORD, and moreover I will not let Israel go.’

searchText: exodus 5:3 and they said: ‘the god of the hebrews hath met with us. let us go, we pray thee, three days’journey into the wilderness, and sacrifice unto the lord our god; lest he fall upon us with pestilence, or with the sword.’
english: And they said: ‘The God of the Hebrews hath met with us. Let us go, we pray thee, three days’journey into the wilderness, and sacrifice unto the LORD our God; lest He fall upon us with pestilence, or with the sword.’

searchText: exodus 5:4 and the king of egypt said unto them: ‘wherefore do ye, moses and aaron, cause the people to break loose from their work? get you unto your burdens.’
english: And the king of Egypt said unto them: ‘Wherefore do ye, Moses and Aaron, cause the people to break loose from their work? get you unto your burdens.’

searchText: exodus 5:5 and pharaoh said: ‘behold, the people of the land are now many, and will ye make them rest from their burdens?’
english: And Pharaoh said: ‘Behold, the people of the land are now many, and will ye make them rest from their burdens?’

searchText: exodus 5:6 and the same day pharaoh commanded the taskmasters of the people, and their officers, saying:
english: And the same day Pharaoh commanded the taskmasters of the people, and their officers, saying:

searchText: exodus 5:7 ’ye shall no more give the people straw to make brick, as heretofore. let them go and gather straw for themselves.
english: ’Ye shall no more give the people straw to make brick, as heretofore. Let them go and gather straw for themselves.

searchText: exodus 5:8 and the tale of the bricks, which they did make heretofore, ye shall lay upon them; ye shall not diminish aught thereof; for they are idle; therefore they cry, saying: let us go and sacrifice to our god.
english: And the tale of the bricks, which they did make heretofore, ye shall lay upon them; ye shall not diminish aught thereof; for they are idle; therefore they cry, saying: Let us go and sacrifice to our God.

searchText: exodus 5:9 let heavier work be laid upon the men, that they may labour therein; and let them not regard lying words.’
english: Let heavier work be laid upon the men, that they may labour therein; and let them not regard lying words.’

searchText: exodus 5:10 and the taskmasters of the people went out, and their officers, and they spoke to the people, saying: ‘thus saith pharaoh: i will not give you straw.
english: And the taskmasters of the people went out, and their officers, and they spoke to the people, saying: ‘Thus saith Pharaoh: I will not give you straw.

searchText: exodus 5:11 go yourselves, get you straw where ye can find it; for nought of your work shall be diminished.’
english: Go yourselves, get you straw where ye can find it; for nought of your work shall be diminished.’

searchText: exodus 5:12 so the people were scattered abroad throughout all the land of egypt to gather stubble for straw.
english: So the people were scattered abroad throughout all the land of Egypt to gather stubble for straw.

searchText: exodus 5:13 and the taskmasters were urgent, saying: ‘fulfil your work, your daily task, as when there was straw.’
english: And the taskmasters were urgent, saying: ‘Fulfil your work, your daily task, as when there was straw.’

searchText: exodus 5:14 and the officers of the children of israel, whom pharaoh’s taskmasters had set over them, were beaten, saying: ‘wherefore have ye not fulfilled your appointed task in making brick both yesterday and today as heretofore?’
english: And the officers of the children of Israel, whom Pharaoh’s taskmasters had set over them, were beaten, saying: ‘Wherefore have ye not fulfilled your appointed task in making brick both yesterday and today as heretofore?’

searchText: exodus 5:15 then the officers of the children of israel came and cried unto pharaoh, saying: ‘wherefore dealest thou thus with thy servants?
english: Then the officers of the children of Israel came and cried unto Pharaoh, saying: ‘Wherefore dealest thou thus with thy servants?

searchText: exodus 5:16 there is no straw given unto thy servants, and they say to us: make brick; and, behold, thy servants are beaten, but the fault is in thine own people.’
english: There is no straw given unto thy servants, and they say to us: Make brick; and, behold, thy servants are beaten, but the fault is in thine own people.’

searchText: exodus 5:17 but he said: ‘ye are idle, ye are idle; therefore ye say: let us go and sacrifice to the lord.
english: But he said: ‘Ye are idle, ye are idle; therefore ye say: Let us go and sacrifice to the LORD.

searchText: exodus 5:18 go therefore now, and work; for there shall no straw be given you, yet shall ye deliver the tale of bricks.’
english: Go therefore now, and work; for there shall no straw be given you, yet shall ye deliver the tale of bricks.’

searchText: exodus 5:19 and the officers of the children of israel did see that they were set on mischief, when they said: ‘ye shall not diminish aught from your bricks, your daily task.’
english: And the officers of the children of Israel did see that they were set on mischief, when they said: ‘Ye shall not diminish aught from your bricks, your daily task.’

searchText: exodus 5:20 and they met moses and aaron, who stood in the way, as they came forth from pharaoh;
english: And they met Moses and Aaron, who stood in the way, as they came forth from Pharaoh;

searchText: exodus 5:21 and they said unto them: ‘the lord look upon you, and judge; because ye have made our savour to be abhorred in the eyes of pharaoh, and in the eyes of his servants, to put a sword in their hand to slay us.’
english: and they said unto them: ‘The LORD look upon you, and judge; because ye have made our savour to be abhorred in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of his servants, to put a sword in their hand to slay us.’

searchText: exodus 5:22 and moses returned unto the lord, and said: ‘lord, wherefore hast thou dealt ill with this people? why is it that thou hast sent me?
english: And Moses returned unto the LORD, and said: ‘Lord, wherefore hast Thou dealt ill with this people? why is it that Thou hast sent me?

searchText: exodus 5:23 for since i came to pharaoh to speak in thy name, he hath dealt ill with this people; neither hast thou delivered thy people at all.’
english: For since I came to Pharaoh to speak in Thy name, he hath dealt ill with this people; neither hast Thou delivered Thy people at all.’

searchText: exodus 6:1 and the lord said unto moses: ‘now shalt thou see what i will do to pharaoh; for by a strong hand shall he let them go, and by a strong hand shall he drive them out of his land.’
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Now shalt thou see what I will do to Pharaoh; for by a strong hand shall he let them go, and by a strong hand shall he drive them out of his land.’

searchText: exodus 6:2 and god spoke unto moses, and said unto him: ‘i am the lord;
english: And God spoke unto Moses, and said unto him: ‘I am the LORD;

searchText: exodus 6:3 and i appeared unto abraham, unto isaac, and unto jacob, as god almighty, but by my name יהוה i made me not known to them.
english: and I appeared unto Abraham, unto Isaac, and unto Jacob, as God Almighty, but by My name יהוה I made Me not known to them.

searchText: exodus 6:4 and i have also established my covenant with them, to give them the land of canaan, the land of their sojournings, wherein they sojourned.
english: And I have also established My covenant with them, to give them the land of Canaan, the land of their sojournings, wherein they sojourned.

searchText: exodus 6:5 and moreover i have heard the groaning of the children of israel, whom the egyptians keep in bondage; and i have remembered my covenant.
english: And moreover I have heard the groaning of the children of Israel, whom the Egyptians keep in bondage; and I have remembered My covenant.

searchText: exodus 6:6 wherefore say unto the children of israel: i am the lord, and i will bring you out from under the burdens of the egyptians, and i will deliver you from their bondage, and i will redeem you with an outstretched arm, and with great judgments;
english: Wherefore say unto the children of Israel: I am the LORD, and I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians, and I will deliver you from their bondage, and I will redeem you with an outstretched arm, and with great judgments;

searchText: exodus 6:7 and i will take you to me for a people, and i will be to you a god; and ye shall know that i am the lord your god, who brought you out from under the burdens of the egyptians.
english: and I will take you to Me for a people, and I will be to you a God; and ye shall know that I am the LORD your God, who brought you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians.

searchText: exodus 6:8 and i will bring you in unto the land, concerning which i lifted up my hand to give it to abraham, to isaac, and to jacob; and i will give it you for a heritage: i am the lord.’
english: And I will bring you in unto the land, concerning which I lifted up My hand to give it to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob; and I will give it you for a heritage: I am the LORD.’

searchText: exodus 6:9 and moses spoke so unto the children of israel; but they hearkened not unto moses for impatience of spirit, and for cruel bondage.
english: And Moses spoke so unto the children of Israel; but they hearkened not unto Moses for impatience of spirit, and for cruel bondage.

searchText: exodus 6:10 and the lord spoke unto moses, saying:
english: And the LORD spoke unto Moses, saying:

searchText: exodus 6:11 ’go in, speak unto pharaoh king of egypt, that he let the children of israel go out of his land.’
english: ’Go in, speak unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land.’

searchText: exodus 6:12 and moses spoke before the lord, saying: ‘behold, the children of israel have not hearkened unto me; how then shall pharaoh hear me, who am of uncircumcised lips?’
english: And Moses spoke before the LORD, saying: ‘Behold, the children of Israel have not hearkened unto me; how then shall Pharaoh hear me, who am of uncircumcised lips?’

searchText: exodus 6:13 and the lord spoke unto moses and unto aaron, and gave them a charge unto the children of israel, and unto pharaoh king of egypt, to bring the children of israel out of the land of egypt.
english: And the LORD spoke unto Moses and unto Aaron, and gave them a charge unto the children of Israel, and unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 6:14 these are the heads of their fathers’houses: the sons of reuben the first-born of israel: hanoch, and pallu, hezron, and carmi. these are the families of reuben.
english: These are the heads of their fathers’houses: the sons of Reuben the first-born of Israel: Hanoch, and Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi. These are the families of Reuben.

searchText: exodus 6:15 and the sons of simeon: jemuel, and jamin, and ohad, and jachin, and zohar, and shaul the son of a canaanitish woman. these are the families of simeon.
english: And the sons of Simeon: Jemuel, and Jamin, and Ohad, and Jachin, and Zohar, and Shaul the son of a Canaanitish woman. These are the families of Simeon.

searchText: exodus 6:16 and these are the names of the sons of levi according to their generations: gershon and kohath, and merari. and the years of the life of levi were a hundred thirty and seven years.
english: And these are the names of the sons of Levi according to their generations: Gershon and Kohath, and Merari. And the years of the life of Levi were a hundred thirty and seven years.

searchText: exodus 6:17 the sons of gershon: libni and shimei, according to their families.
english: The sons of Gershon: Libni and Shimei, according to their families.

searchText: exodus 6:18 and the sons of kohath: amram, and izhar, and hebron, and uzziel. and the years of the life of kohath were a hundred thirty and three years.
english: And the sons of Kohath: Amram, and Izhar, and Hebron, and Uzziel. And the years of the life of Kohath were a hundred thirty and three years.

searchText: exodus 6:19 and the sons of merari: mahli and mushi. these are the families of the levites according to their generations.
english: And the sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their generations.

searchText: exodus 6:20 and amram took him jochebed his father’s sister to wife; and she bore him aaron and moses. and the years of the life of amram were a hundred and thirty and seven years.
english: And Amram took him Jochebed his father’s sister to wife; and she bore him Aaron and Moses. And the years of the life of Amram were a hundred and thirty and seven years.

searchText: exodus 6:21 and the sons of izhar: korah, and nepheg, and zichri.
english: And the sons of Izhar: Korah, and Nepheg, and Zichri.

searchText: exodus 6:22 and the sons of uzziel: mishael, and elzaphan, and sithri.
english: And the sons of Uzziel: Mishael, and Elzaphan, and Sithri.

searchText: exodus 6:23 and aaron took him elisheba, the daughter of amminadab, the sister of nahshon, to wife; and she bore him nadab and abihu, eleazar and ithamar.
english: And Aaron took him Elisheba, the daughter of Amminadab, the sister of Nahshon, to wife; and she bore him Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.

searchText: exodus 6:24 and the sons of korah: assir, and elkanah, and abiasaph; these are the families of the korahites.
english: And the sons of Korah: Assir, and Elkanah, and Abiasaph; these are the families of the Korahites.

searchText: exodus 6:25 and eleazar aaron’s son took him one of the daughters of putiel to wife; and she bore him phinehas. these are the heads of the fathers’houses of the levites according to their families.
english: And Eleazar Aaron’s son took him one of the daughters of Putiel to wife; and she bore him Phinehas. These are the heads of the fathers’houses of the Levites according to their families.

searchText: exodus 6:26 these are that aaron and moses, to whom the lord said: ‘bring out the children of israel from the land of egypt according to their hosts.’
english: These are that Aaron and Moses, to whom the LORD said: ‘Bring out the children of Israel from the land of Egypt according to their hosts.’

searchText: exodus 6:27 these are they that spoke to pharaoh king of egypt, to bring out the children of israel from egypt. these are that moses and aaron.
english: These are they that spoke to Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring out the children of Israel from Egypt. These are that Moses and Aaron.

searchText: exodus 6:28 and it came to pass on the day when the lord spoke unto moses in the land of egypt,
english: And it came to pass on the day when the LORD spoke unto Moses in the land of Egypt,

searchText: exodus 6:29 that the lord spoke unto moses, saying: ‘i am the lord; speak thou unto pharaoh king of egypt all that i speak unto thee.’
english: that the LORD spoke unto Moses, saying: ‘I am the LORD; speak thou unto Pharaoh king of Egypt all that I speak unto thee.’

searchText: exodus 6:30 and moses said before the lord: ‘behold, i am of uncircumcised lips, and how shall pharaoh hearken unto me?’
english: And Moses said before the LORD: ‘Behold, I am of uncircumcised lips, and how shall Pharaoh hearken unto me?’

searchText: exodus 7:1 and the lord said unto moses: ‘see, i have set thee in god’s stead to pharaoh; and aaron thy brother shall be thy prophet.
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘See, I have set thee in God’s stead to Pharaoh; and Aaron thy brother shall be thy prophet.

searchText: exodus 7:2 thou shalt speak all that i command thee; and aaron thy brother shall speak unto pharaoh, that he let the children of israel go out of his land.
english: Thou shalt speak all that I command thee; and Aaron thy brother shall speak unto Pharaoh, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land.

searchText: exodus 7:3 and i will harden pharaoh’s heart, and multiply my signs and my wonders in the land of egypt.
english: And I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, and multiply My signs and My wonders in the land of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 7:4 but pharaoh will not hearken unto you, and i will lay my hand upon egypt, and bring forth my hosts, my people the children of israel, out of the land of egypt, by great judgments.
english: But Pharaoh will not hearken unto you, and I will lay My hand upon Egypt, and bring forth My hosts, My people the children of Israel, out of the land of Egypt, by great judgments.

searchText: exodus 7:5 and the egyptians shall know that i am the lord, when i stretch forth my hand upon egypt, and bring out the children of israel from among them.’
english: And the Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD, when I stretch forth My hand upon Egypt, and bring out the children of Israel from among them.’

searchText: exodus 7:6 and moses and aaron did so; as the lord commanded them, so did they.
english: And Moses and Aaron did so; as the LORD commanded them, so did they.

searchText: exodus 7:7 and moses was fourscore years old, and aaron fourscore and three years old, when they spoke unto pharaoh.
english: And Moses was fourscore years old, and Aaron fourscore and three years old, when they spoke unto Pharaoh.

searchText: exodus 7:8 and the lord spoke unto moses and unto aaron, saying:
english: And the LORD spoke unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying:

searchText: exodus 7:9 ’when pharaoh shall speak unto you, saying: show a wonder for you; then thou shalt say unto aaron: take thy rod, and cast it down before pharaoh, that it become a serpent.’
english: ’When Pharaoh shall speak unto you, saying: Show a wonder for you; then thou shalt say unto Aaron: Take thy rod, and cast it down before Pharaoh, that it become a serpent.’

searchText: exodus 7:10 and moses and aaron went in unto pharaoh, and they did so, as the lord had commanded; and aaron cast down his rod before pharaoh and before his servants, and it became a serpent.
english: And Moses and Aaron went in unto Pharaoh, and they did so, as the LORD had commanded; and Aaron cast down his rod before Pharaoh and before his servants, and it became a serpent.

searchText: exodus 7:11 then pharaoh also called for the wise men and the sorcerers; and they also, the magicians of egypt, did in like manner with their secret arts.
english: Then Pharaoh also called for the wise men and the sorcerers; and they also, the magicians of Egypt, did in like manner with their secret arts.

searchText: exodus 7:12 for they cast down every man his rod, and they became serpents; but aaron’s rod swallowed up their rods.
english: For they cast down every man his rod, and they became serpents; but Aaron’s rod swallowed up their rods.

searchText: exodus 7:13 and pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he hearkened not unto them; as the lord had spoken.
english: And Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he hearkened not unto them; as the LORD had spoken.

searchText: exodus 7:14 and the lord said unto moses: ‘pharaoh’s heart is stubborn, he refuseth to let the people go.
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Pharaoh’s heart is stubborn, he refuseth to let the people go.

searchText: exodus 7:15 get thee unto pharaoh in the morning; lo, he goeth out unto the water; and thou shalt stand by the river’s brink to meet him; and the rod which was turned to a serpent shalt thou take in thy hand.
english: Get thee unto Pharaoh in the morning; lo, he goeth out unto the water; and thou shalt stand by the river’s brink to meet him; and the rod which was turned to a serpent shalt thou take in thy hand.

searchText: exodus 7:16 and thou shalt say unto him: the lord, the god of the hebrews, hath sent me unto thee, saying: let my people go, that they may serve me in the wilderness; and, behold, hitherto thou hast not hearkened;
english: And thou shalt say unto him: The LORD, the God of the Hebrews, hath sent me unto thee, saying: Let My people go, that they may serve Me in the wilderness; and, behold, hitherto thou hast not hearkened;

searchText: exodus 7:17 thus saith the lord: in this thou shalt know that i am the lord—behold, i will smite with the rod that is in my hand upon the waters which are in the river, and they shall be turned to blood.
english: thus saith the LORD: In this thou shalt know that I am the LORD—behold, I will smite with the rod that is in my hand upon the waters which are in the river, and they shall be turned to blood.

searchText: exodus 7:18 and the fish that are in the river shall die, and the river shall become foul; and the egyptians shall loathe to drink water from the river.’
english: And the fish that are in the river shall die, and the river shall become foul; and the Egyptians shall loathe to drink water from the river.’

searchText: exodus 7:19 and the lord said unto moses: ‘say unto aaron: take thy rod, and stretch out thy hand over the waters of egypt, over their rivers, over their streams, and over their pools, and over all their ponds of water, that they may become blood; and there shall be blood throughout all the land of egypt, both in vessels of wood and in vessels of stone.’
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Say unto Aaron: Take thy rod, and stretch out thy hand over the waters of Egypt, over their rivers, over their streams, and over their pools, and over all their ponds of water, that they may become blood; and there shall be blood throughout all the land of Egypt, both in vessels of wood and in vessels of stone.’

searchText: exodus 7:20 and moses and aaron did so, as the lord commanded; and he lifted up the rod, and smote the waters that were in the river, in the sight of pharaoh, and in the sight of his servants; and all the waters that were in the river were turned to blood.
english: And Moses and Aaron did so, as the LORD commanded; and he lifted up the rod, and smote the waters that were in the river, in the sight of Pharaoh, and in the sight of his servants; and all the waters that were in the river were turned to blood.

searchText: exodus 7:21 and the fish that were in the river died; and the river became foul, and the egyptians could not drink water from the river; and the blood was throughout all the land of egypt.
english: And the fish that were in the river died; and the river became foul, and the Egyptians could not drink water from the river; and the blood was throughout all the land of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 7:22 and the magicians of egypt did in like manner with their secret arts; and pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he hearkened not unto them; as the lord had spoken.
english: And the magicians of Egypt did in like manner with their secret arts; and Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he hearkened not unto them; as the LORD had spoken.

searchText: exodus 7:23 and pharaoh turned and went into his house, neither did he lay even this to heart.
english: And Pharaoh turned and went into his house, neither did he lay even this to heart.

searchText: exodus 7:24 and all the egyptians digged round about the river for water to drink; for they could not drink of the water of the river.
english: And all the Egyptians digged round about the river for water to drink; for they could not drink of the water of the river.

searchText: exodus 7:25 and seven days were fulfilled, after that the lord had smitten the river.
english: And seven days were fulfilled, after that the LORD had smitten the river.

searchText: exodus 7:26 and the lord spoke unto moses: ‘go in unto pharaoh, and say unto him: thus saith the lord: let my people go, that they may serve me.
english: And the LORD spoke unto Moses: ‘Go in unto Pharaoh, and say unto him: Thus saith the LORD: Let My people go, that they may serve Me.

searchText: exodus 7:27 and if thou refuse to let them go, behold, i will smite all thy borders with frogs.
english: And if thou refuse to let them go, behold, I will smite all thy borders with frogs.

searchText: exodus 7:28 and the river shall swarm with frogs, which shall go up and come into thy house, and into thy bed-chamber, and upon thy bed, and into the house of thy servants, and upon thy people, and into thine ovens, and into thy kneading-troughs.
english: And the river shall swarm with frogs, which shall go up and come into thy house, and into thy bed-chamber, and upon thy bed, and into the house of thy servants, and upon thy people, and into thine ovens, and into thy kneading-troughs.

searchText: exodus 7:29 and the frogs shall come up both upon thee, and upon thy people, and upon all thy servants.’
english: And the frogs shall come up both upon thee, and upon thy people, and upon all thy servants.’

searchText: exodus 8:1 and the lord said unto moses: ‘say unto aaron: stretch forth thy hand with thy rod over the rivers, over the canals, and over the pools, and cause frogs to come up upon the land of egypt.’
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Say unto Aaron: Stretch forth thy hand with thy rod over the rivers, over the canals, and over the pools, and cause frogs to come up upon the land of Egypt.’

searchText: exodus 8:2 and aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of egypt; and the frogs came up, and covered the land of egypt.
english: And Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt; and the frogs came up, and covered the land of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 8:3 and the magicians did in like manner with their secret arts, and brought up frogs upon the land of egypt.
english: And the magicians did in like manner with their secret arts, and brought up frogs upon the land of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 8:4 then pharaoh called for moses and aaron, and said: ‘entreat the lord, that he take away the frogs from me, and from my people; and i will let the people go, that they may sacrifice unto the lord.’
english: Then Pharaoh called for Moses and Aaron, and said: ‘Entreat the LORD, that He take away the frogs from me, and from my people; and I will let the people go, that they may sacrifice unto the LORD.’

searchText: exodus 8:5 and moses said unto pharaoh: ‘have thou this glory over me; against what time shall i entreat for thee, and for thy servants, and for thy people, that the frogs be destroyed from thee and thy houses, and remain in the river only?’
english: And Moses said unto Pharaoh: ‘Have thou this glory over me; against what time shall I entreat for thee, and for thy servants, and for thy people, that the frogs be destroyed from thee and thy houses, and remain in the river only?’

searchText: exodus 8:6 and he said: ‘against to-morrow.’ and he said: ‘be it according to thy word; that thou mayest know that there is none like unto the lord our god.
english: And he said: ‘Against to-morrow.’ And he said: ‘Be it according to thy word; that thou mayest know that there is none like unto the LORD our God.

searchText: exodus 8:7 and the frogs shall depart from thee, and from thy houses, and from thy servants, and from thy people; they shall remain in the river only.’
english: And the frogs shall depart from thee, and from thy houses, and from thy servants, and from thy people; they shall remain in the river only.’

searchText: exodus 8:8 and moses and aaron went out from pharaoh; and moses cried unto the lord concerning the frogs, which he had brought upon pharaoh.
english: And Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh; and Moses cried unto the LORD concerning the frogs, which He had brought upon Pharaoh.

searchText: exodus 8:9 and the lord did according to the word of moses; and the frogs died out of the houses, out of the courts, and out of the fields.
english: And the LORD did according to the word of Moses; and the frogs died out of the houses, out of the courts, and out of the fields.

searchText: exodus 8:10 and they gathered them together in heaps; and the land stank.
english: And they gathered them together in heaps; and the land stank.

searchText: exodus 8:11 but when pharaoh saw that there was respite, he hardened his heart, and hearkened not unto them; as the lord had spoken.
english: But when Pharaoh saw that there was respite, he hardened his heart, and hearkened not unto them; as the LORD had spoken.

searchText: exodus 8:12 and the lord said unto moses: ‘say unto aaron: stretch out thy rod, and smite the dust of the earth, that it may become gnats throughout all the land of egypt.’
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Say unto Aaron: Stretch out thy rod, and smite the dust of the earth, that it may become gnats throughout all the land of Egypt.’

searchText: exodus 8:13 and they did so; and aaron stretched out his hand with his rod, and smote the dust of the earth, and there were gnats upon man, and upon beast; all the dust of the earth became gnats throughout all the land of egypt.
english: And they did so; and Aaron stretched out his hand with his rod, and smote the dust of the earth, and there were gnats upon man, and upon beast; all the dust of the earth became gnats throughout all the land of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 8:14 and the magicians did so with their secret arts to bring forth gnats, but they could not; and there were gnats upon man, and upon beast.
english: And the magicians did so with their secret arts to bring forth gnats, but they could not; and there were gnats upon man, and upon beast.

searchText: exodus 8:15 then the magicians said unto pharaoh: ‘this is the finger of god’; and pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he hearkened not unto them; as the lord had spoken.
english: Then the magicians said unto Pharaoh: ‘This is the finger of God’; and Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he hearkened not unto them; as the LORD had spoken.

searchText: exodus 8:16 and the lord said unto moses: ‘rise up early in the morning, and stand before pharaoh; lo, he cometh forth to the water; and say unto him: thus saith the lord: let my people go, that they may serve me.
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Rise up early in the morning, and stand before Pharaoh; lo, he cometh forth to the water; and say unto him: Thus saith the LORD: Let My people go, that they may serve Me.

searchText: exodus 8:17 else, if thou wilt not let my people go, behold, i will send swarms of flies upon thee, and upon thy servants, and upon thy people, and into thy houses; and the houses of the egyptians shall be full of swarms of flies, and also the ground whereon they are.
english: Else, if thou wilt not let My people go, behold, I will send swarms of flies upon thee, and upon thy servants, and upon thy people, and into thy houses; and the houses of the Egyptians shall be full of swarms of flies, and also the ground whereon they are.

searchText: exodus 8:18 and i will set apart in that day the land of goshen, in which my people dwell, that no swarms of flies shall be there; to the end that thou mayest know that i am the lord in the midst of the earth.
english: And I will set apart in that day the land of Goshen, in which My people dwell, that no swarms of flies shall be there; to the end that thou mayest know that I am the LORD in the midst of the earth.

searchText: exodus 8:19 and i will put a division between my people and thy people—by to-morrow shall this sign be.’
english: And I will put a division between My people and thy people—by to-morrow shall this sign be.’

searchText: exodus 8:20 and the lord did so; and there came grievous swarms of flies into the house of pharaoh, and into his servants’houses; and in all the land of egypt the land was ruined by reason of the swarms of flies.
english: And the LORD did so; and there came grievous swarms of flies into the house of Pharaoh, and into his servants’houses; and in all the land of Egypt the land was ruined by reason of the swarms of flies.

searchText: exodus 8:21 and pharaoh called for moses and for aaron, and said: ‘go ye, sacrifice to your god in the land.’
english: And Pharaoh called for Moses and for Aaron, and said: ‘Go ye, sacrifice to your God in the land.’

searchText: exodus 8:22 and moses said: ‘it is not meet so to do; for we shall sacrifice the abomination of the egyptians to the lord our god; lo, if we sacrifice the abomination of the egyptians before their eyes, will they not stone us?
english: And Moses said: ‘It is not meet so to do; for we shall sacrifice the abomination of the Egyptians to the LORD our God; lo, if we sacrifice the abomination of the Egyptians before their eyes, will they not stone us?

searchText: exodus 8:23 we will go three days’journey into the wilderness, and sacrifice to the lord our god, as he shall command us.’
english: We will go three days’journey into the wilderness, and sacrifice to the LORD our God, as He shall command us.’

searchText: exodus 8:24 and pharaoh said: ‘i will let you go, that ye may sacrifice to the lord your god in the wilderness; only ye shall not go very far away; entreat for me.’
english: And Pharaoh said: ‘I will let you go, that ye may sacrifice to the LORD your God in the wilderness; only ye shall not go very far away; entreat for me.’

searchText: exodus 8:25 and moses said: ‘behold, i go out from thee, and i will entreat the lord that the swarms of flies may depart from pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people, tomorrow; only let not pharaoh deal deceitfully any more in not letting the people go to sacrifice to the lord.’
english: And Moses said: ‘Behold, I go out from thee, and I will entreat the LORD that the swarms of flies may depart from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people, tomorrow; only let not Pharaoh deal deceitfully any more in not letting the people go to sacrifice to the LORD.’

searchText: exodus 8:26 and moses went out from pharaoh, and entreated the lord.
english: And Moses went out from Pharaoh, and entreated the LORD.

searchText: exodus 8:27 and the lord did according to the word of moses; and he removed the swarms of flies from pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people; there remained not one.
english: And the LORD did according to the word of Moses; and He removed the swarms of flies from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people; there remained not one.

searchText: exodus 8:28 and pharaoh hardened his heart this time also, and he did not let the people go.
english: And Pharaoh hardened his heart this time also, and he did not let the people go.

searchText: exodus 9:1 then the lord said unto moses: ‘go in unto pharaoh, and tell him: thus saith the lord, the god of the hebrews: let my people go, that they may serve me.
english: Then the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Go in unto Pharaoh, and tell him: Thus saith the LORD, the God of the Hebrews: Let My people go, that they may serve Me.

searchText: exodus 9:2 for if thou refuse to let them go, and wilt hold them still,
english: For if thou refuse to let them go, and wilt hold them still,

searchText: exodus 9:3 behold, the hand of the lord is upon thy cattle which are in the field, upon the horses, upon the asses, upon the camels, upon the herds, and upon the flocks; there shall be a very grievous murrain.
english: behold, the hand of the LORD is upon thy cattle which are in the field, upon the horses, upon the asses, upon the camels, upon the herds, and upon the flocks; there shall be a very grievous murrain.

searchText: exodus 9:4 and the lord shall make a division between the cattle of israel and the cattle of egypt; and there shall nothing die of all that belongeth to the children of israel.’
english: And the LORD shall make a division between the cattle of Israel and the cattle of Egypt; and there shall nothing die of all that belongeth to the children of Israel.’

searchText: exodus 9:5 and the lord appointed a set time, saying: ‘tomorrow the lord shall do this thing in the land.’
english: And the LORD appointed a set time, saying: ‘Tomorrow the LORD shall do this thing in the land.’

searchText: exodus 9:6 and the lord did that thing on the morrow, and all the cattle of egypt died; but of the cattle of the children of israel died not one.
english: And the LORD did that thing on the morrow, and all the cattle of Egypt died; but of the cattle of the children of Israel died not one.

searchText: exodus 9:7 and pharaoh sent, and, behold, there was not so much as one of the cattle of the israelites dead. but the heart of pharaoh was stubborn, and he did not let the people go.
english: And Pharaoh sent, and, behold, there was not so much as one of the cattle of the Israelites dead. But the heart of Pharaoh was stubborn, and he did not let the people go.

searchText: exodus 9:8 and the lord said unto moses and unto aaron: ‘take to you handfuls of soot of the furnace, and let moses throw it heavenward in the sight of pharaoh.
english: And the LORD said unto Moses and unto Aaron: ‘Take to you handfuls of soot of the furnace, and let Moses throw it heavenward in the sight of Pharaoh.

searchText: exodus 9:9 and it shall become small dust over all the land of egypt, and shall be a boil breaking forth with blains upon man and upon beast, throughout all the land of egypt.’
english: And it shall become small dust over all the land of Egypt, and shall be a boil breaking forth with blains upon man and upon beast, throughout all the land of Egypt.’

searchText: exodus 9:10 and they took soot of the furnace, and stood before pharaoh; and moses threw it up heavenward; and it became a boil breaking forth with blains upon man and upon beast.
english: And they took soot of the furnace, and stood before Pharaoh; and Moses threw it up heavenward; and it became a boil breaking forth with blains upon man and upon beast.

searchText: exodus 9:11 and the magicians could not stand before moses because of the boils; for the boils were upon the magicians, and upon all the egyptians.
english: And the magicians could not stand before Moses because of the boils; for the boils were upon the magicians, and upon all the Egyptians.

searchText: exodus 9:12 and the lord hardened the heart of pharaoh, and he hearkened not unto them; as the lord had spoken unto moses.
english: And the LORD hardened the heart of Pharaoh, and he hearkened not unto them; as the LORD had spoken unto Moses.

searchText: exodus 9:13 and the lord said unto moses: ‘rise up early in the morning, and stand before pharaoh, and say unto him: thus saith the lord, the god of the hebrews: let my people go, that they may serve me.
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Rise up early in the morning, and stand before Pharaoh, and say unto him: Thus saith the LORD, the God of the Hebrews: Let My people go, that they may serve Me.

searchText: exodus 9:14 for i will this time send all my plagues upon thy person, and upon thy servants, and upon thy people; that thou mayest know that there is none like me in all the earth.
english: For I will this time send all My plagues upon thy person, and upon thy servants, and upon thy people; that thou mayest know that there is none like Me in all the earth.

searchText: exodus 9:15 surely now i had put forth my hand, and smitten thee and thy people with pestilence, and thou hadst been cut off from the earth.
english: Surely now I had put forth My hand, and smitten thee and thy people with pestilence, and thou hadst been cut off from the earth.

searchText: exodus 9:16 but in very deed for this cause have i made thee to stand, to show thee my power, and that my name may be declared throughout all the earth.
english: But in very deed for this cause have I made thee to stand, to show thee My power, and that My name may be declared throughout all the earth.

searchText: exodus 9:17 as yet exaltest thou thyself against my people, that thou wilt not let them go?
english: As yet exaltest thou thyself against My people, that thou wilt not let them go?

searchText: exodus 9:18 behold, tomorrow about this time i will cause it to rain a very grievous hail, such as hath not been in egypt since the day it was founded even until now.
english: Behold, tomorrow about this time I will cause it to rain a very grievous hail, such as hath not been in Egypt since the day it was founded even until now.

searchText: exodus 9:19 now therefore send, hasten in thy cattle and all that thou hast in the field; for every man and beast that shall be found in the field, and shall not be brought home, the hail shall come down upon them, and they shall die.’
english: Now therefore send, hasten in thy cattle and all that thou hast in the field; for every man and beast that shall be found in the field, and shall not be brought home, the hail shall come down upon them, and they shall die.’

searchText: exodus 9:20 he that feared the word of the lord among the servants of pharaoh made his servants and his cattle flee into the houses;
english: He that feared the word of the LORD among the servants of Pharaoh made his servants and his cattle flee into the houses;

searchText: exodus 9:21 and he that regarded not the word of the lord left his servants and his cattle in the field.
english: and he that regarded not the word of the LORD left his servants and his cattle in the field.

searchText: exodus 9:22 and the lord said unto moses: ‘stretch forth thy hand toward heaven, that there may be hail in all the land of egypt, upon man, and upon beast, and upon every herb of the field, throughout the land of egypt.’
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Stretch forth thy hand toward heaven, that there may be hail in all the land of Egypt, upon man, and upon beast, and upon every herb of the field, throughout the land of Egypt.’

searchText: exodus 9:23 and moses stretched forth his rod toward heaven; and the lord sent thunder and hail, and fire ran down unto the earth; and the lord caused to hail upon the land of egypt.
english: And Moses stretched forth his rod toward heaven; and the LORD sent thunder and hail, and fire ran down unto the earth; and the LORD caused to hail upon the land of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 9:24 so there was hail, and fire flashing up amidst the hail, very grievous, such as had not been in all the land of egypt since it became a nation.
english: So there was hail, and fire flashing up amidst the hail, very grievous, such as had not been in all the land of Egypt since it became a nation.

searchText: exodus 9:25 and the hail smote throughout all the land of egypt all that was in the field, both man and beast; and the hail smote every herb of the field, and broke every tree of the field.
english: And the hail smote throughout all the land of Egypt all that was in the field, both man and beast; and the hail smote every herb of the field, and broke every tree of the field.

searchText: exodus 9:26 only in the land of goshen, where the children of israel were, was there no hail.
english: Only in the land of Goshen, where the children of Israel were, was there no hail.

searchText: exodus 9:27 and pharaoh sent, and called for moses and aaron, and said unto them: ‘i have sinned this time; the lord is righteous, and i and my people are wicked.
english: And Pharaoh sent, and called for Moses and Aaron, and said unto them: ‘I have sinned this time; the LORD is righteous, and I and my people are wicked.

searchText: exodus 9:28 entreat the lord, and let there be enough of these mighty thunderings and hail; and i will let you go, and ye shall stay no longer.’
english: Entreat the LORD, and let there be enough of these mighty thunderings and hail; and I will let you go, and ye shall stay no longer.’

searchText: exodus 9:29 and moses said unto him: ‘as soon as i am gone out of the city, i will spread forth my hands unto the lord; the thunders shall cease, neither shall there be any more hail; that thou mayest know that the earth is the lord’s.
english: And Moses said unto him: ‘As soon as I am gone out of the city, I will spread forth my hands unto the LORD; the thunders shall cease, neither shall there be any more hail; that thou mayest know that the earth is the LORD’s.

searchText: exodus 9:30 but as for thee and thy servants, i know that ye will not yet fear the lord god.’—
english: But as for thee and thy servants, I know that ye will not yet fear the LORD God.’—

searchText: exodus 9:31 and the flax and the barley were smitten; for the barley was in the ear, and the flax was in bloom.
english: And the flax and the barley were smitten; for the barley was in the ear, and the flax was in bloom.

searchText: exodus 9:32 but the wheat and the spelt were not smitten; for they ripen late.—
english: But the wheat and the spelt were not smitten; for they ripen late.—

searchText: exodus 9:33 and moses went out of the city from pharaoh, and spread forth his hands unto the lord; and the thunders and hail ceased, and the rain was not poured upon the earth.
english: And Moses went out of the city from Pharaoh, and spread forth his hands unto the LORD; and the thunders and hail ceased, and the rain was not poured upon the earth.

searchText: exodus 9:34 and when pharaoh saw that the rain and the hail and the thunders were ceased, he sinned yet more, and hardened his heart, he and his servants.
english: And when Pharaoh saw that the rain and the hail and the thunders were ceased, he sinned yet more, and hardened his heart, he and his servants.

searchText: exodus 9:35 and the heart of pharaoh was hardened, and he did not let the children of israel go; as the lord had spoken by moses.
english: And the heart of Pharaoh was hardened, and he did not let the children of Israel go; as the LORD had spoken by Moses.

searchText: exodus 10:1 and the lord said unto moses: ‘go in unto pharaoh; for i have hardened his heart, and the heart of his servants, that i might show these my signs in the midst of them;
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Go in unto Pharaoh; for I have hardened his heart, and the heart of his servants, that I might show these My signs in the midst of them;

searchText: exodus 10:2 and that thou mayest tell in the ears of thy son, and of thy son’s son, what i have wrought upon egypt, and my signs which i have done among them; that ye may know that i am the lord.’
english: and that thou mayest tell in the ears of thy son, and of thy son’s son, what I have wrought upon Egypt, and My signs which I have done among them; that ye may know that I am the LORD.’

searchText: exodus 10:3 and moses and aaron went in unto pharaoh, and said unto him: ‘thus saith the lord, the god of the hebrews: how long wilt thou refuse to humble thyself before me? let my people go, that they may serve me.
english: And Moses and Aaron went in unto Pharaoh, and said unto him: ‘Thus saith the LORD, the God of the Hebrews: How long wilt thou refuse to humble thyself before Me? let My people go, that they may serve Me.

searchText: exodus 10:4 else, if thou refuse to let my people go, behold, to-morrow will i bring locusts into thy border;
english: Else, if thou refuse to let My people go, behold, to-morrow will I bring locusts into thy border;

searchText: exodus 10:5 and they shall cover the face of the earth, that one shall not be able to see the earth; and they shall eat the residue of that which is escaped, which remaineth unto you from the hail, and shall eat every tree which groweth for you out of the field;
english: and they shall cover the face of the earth, that one shall not be able to see the earth; and they shall eat the residue of that which is escaped, which remaineth unto you from the hail, and shall eat every tree which groweth for you out of the field;

searchText: exodus 10:6 and thy houses shall be filled, and the houses of all thy servants, and the houses of all the egyptians; as neither thy fathers nor thy fathers’fathers have seen, since the day that they were upon the earth unto this day.’ and he turned, and went out from pharaoh.
english: and thy houses shall be filled, and the houses of all thy servants, and the houses of all the Egyptians; as neither thy fathers nor thy fathers’fathers have seen, since the day that they were upon the earth unto this day.’ And he turned, and went out from Pharaoh.

searchText: exodus 10:7 and pharaoh’s servants said unto him: ‘how long shall this man be a snare unto us? let the men go, that they may serve the lord their god, knowest thou not yet that egypt is destroyed?’
english: And Pharaoh’s servants said unto him: ‘How long shall this man be a snare unto us? let the men go, that they may serve the LORD their God, knowest thou not yet that Egypt is destroyed?’

searchText: exodus 10:8 and moses and aaron were brought again unto pharaoh; and he said unto them: ‘go, serve the lord your god; but who are they that shall go?’
english: And Moses and Aaron were brought again unto Pharaoh; and he said unto them: ‘Go, serve the LORD your God; but who are they that shall go?’

searchText: exodus 10:9 and moses said: ‘we will go with our young and with our old, with our sons and with our daughters, with our flocks and with our herds we will go; for we must hold a feast unto the lord.’
english: And Moses said: ‘We will go with our young and with our old, with our sons and with our daughters, with our flocks and with our herds we will go; for we must hold a feast unto the LORD.’

searchText: exodus 10:10 and he said unto them: ‘so be the lord with you, as i will let you go, and your little ones; see ye that evil is before your face.
english: And he said unto them: ‘So be the LORD with you, as I will let you go, and your little ones; see ye that evil is before your face.

searchText: exodus 10:11 not so; go now ye that are men, and serve the lord; for that is what ye desire.’ and they were driven out from pharaoh’s presence.
english: Not so; go now ye that are men, and serve the LORD; for that is what ye desire.’ And they were driven out from Pharaoh’s presence.

searchText: exodus 10:12 and the lord said unto moses: ‘stretch out thy hand over the land of egypt for the locusts, that they may come up upon the land of egypt, and eat every herb of the land, even all that the hail hath left.’
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Stretch out thy hand over the land of Egypt for the locusts, that they may come up upon the land of Egypt, and eat every herb of the land, even all that the hail hath left.’

searchText: exodus 10:13 and moses stretched forth his rod over the land of egypt, and the lord brought an east wind upon the land all that day, and all the night; and when it was morning, the east wind brought the locusts.
english: And Moses stretched forth his rod over the land of Egypt, and the LORD brought an east wind upon the land all that day, and all the night; and when it was morning, the east wind brought the locusts.

searchText: exodus 10:14 and the locusts went up over all the land of egypt, and rested in all the borders of egypt; very grievous were they; before them there were no such locusts as they, neither after them shall be such.
english: And the locusts went up over all the land of Egypt, and rested in all the borders of Egypt; very grievous were they; before them there were no such locusts as they, neither after them shall be such.

searchText: exodus 10:15 for they covered the face of the whole earth, so that the land was darkened; and they did eat every herb of the land, and all the fruit of the trees which the hail had left; and there remained not any green thing, either tree or herb of the field, through all the land of egypt.
english: For they covered the face of the whole earth, so that the land was darkened; and they did eat every herb of the land, and all the fruit of the trees which the hail had left; and there remained not any green thing, either tree or herb of the field, through all the land of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 10:16 then pharaoh called for moses and aaron in haste; and he said: ‘i have sinned against the lord your god, and against you.
english: Then Pharaoh called for Moses and Aaron in haste; and he said: ‘I have sinned against the LORD your God, and against you.

searchText: exodus 10:17 now therefore forgive, i pray thee, my sin only this once, and entreat the lord your god, that he may take away from me this death only.’
english: Now therefore forgive, I pray thee, my sin only this once, and entreat the LORD your God, that He may take away from me this death only.’

searchText: exodus 10:18 and he went out from pharaoh, and entreated the lord.
english: And he went out from Pharaoh, and entreated the LORD.

searchText: exodus 10:19 and the lord turned an exceeding strong west wind, which took up the locusts, and drove them into the red sea; there remained not one locust in all the border of egypt.
english: And the LORD turned an exceeding strong west wind, which took up the locusts, and drove them into the Red Sea; there remained not one locust in all the border of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 10:20 but the lord hardened pharaoh’s heart, and he did not let the children of israel go.
english: But the LORD hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he did not let the children of Israel go.

searchText: exodus 10:21 and the lord said unto moses: ‘stretch out thy hand toward heaven, that there may be darkness over the land of egypt, even darkness which may be felt.’
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Stretch out thy hand toward heaven, that there may be darkness over the land of Egypt, even darkness which may be felt.’

searchText: exodus 10:22 and moses stretched forth his hand toward heaven; and there was a thick darkness in all the land of egypt three days;
english: And Moses stretched forth his hand toward heaven; and there was a thick darkness in all the land of Egypt three days;

searchText: exodus 10:23 they saw not one another, neither rose any from his place for three days; but all the children of israel had light in their dwellings.
english: they saw not one another, neither rose any from his place for three days; but all the children of Israel had light in their dwellings.

searchText: exodus 10:24 and pharaoh called unto moses, and said: ‘go ye, serve the lord; only let your flocks and your herds be stayed; let your little ones also go with you.’
english: And Pharaoh called unto Moses, and said: ‘Go ye, serve the LORD; only let your flocks and your herds be stayed; let your little ones also go with you.’

searchText: exodus 10:25 and moses said: ‘thou must also give into our hand sacrifices and burnt-offerings, that we may sacrifice unto the lord our god.
english: And Moses said: ‘Thou must also give into our hand sacrifices and burnt-offerings, that we may sacrifice unto the LORD our God.

searchText: exodus 10:26 our cattle also shall go with us; there shall not a hoof be left behind; for thereof must we take to serve the lord our god; and we know not with what we must serve the lord, until we come thither.’
english: Our cattle also shall go with us; there shall not a hoof be left behind; for thereof must we take to serve the LORD our God; and we know not with what we must serve the LORD, until we come thither.’

searchText: exodus 10:27 but the lord hardened pharaoh’s heart, and he would not let them go.
english: But the LORD hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he would not let them go.

searchText: exodus 10:28 and pharaoh said unto him: ‘get thee from me, take heed to thyself, see my face no more; for in the day thou seest my face thou shalt die.’
english: And Pharaoh said unto him: ‘Get thee from me, take heed to thyself, see my face no more; for in the day thou seest my face thou shalt die.’

searchText: exodus 10:29 and moses said: ‘thou hast spoken well; i will see thy face again no more.’
english: And Moses said: ‘Thou hast spoken well; I will see thy face again no more.’

searchText: exodus 11:1 and the lord said unto moses: ‘yet one plague more will i bring upon pharaoh, and upon egypt; afterwards he will let you go hence; when he shall let you go, he shall surely thrust you out hence altogether.
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Yet one plague more will I bring upon Pharaoh, and upon Egypt; afterwards he will let you go hence; when he shall let you go, he shall surely thrust you out hence altogether.

searchText: exodus 11:2 speak now in the ears of the people, and let them ask every man of his neighbour, and every woman of her neighbour, jewels of silver, and jewels of gold.’
english: Speak now in the ears of the people, and let them ask every man of his neighbour, and every woman of her neighbour, jewels of silver, and jewels of gold.’

searchText: exodus 11:3 and the lord gave the people favour in the sight of the egyptians. moreover the man moses was very great in the land of egypt, in the sight of pharaoh’s servants, and in the sight of the people.
english: And the LORD gave the people favour in the sight of the Egyptians. Moreover the man Moses was very great in the land of Egypt, in the sight of Pharaoh’s servants, and in the sight of the people.

searchText: exodus 11:4 and moses said: ‘thus saith the lord: about midnight will i go out into the midst of egypt;
english: And Moses said: ‘Thus saith the LORD: About midnight will I go out into the midst of Egypt;

searchText: exodus 11:5 and all the first-born in the land of egypt shall die, from the first-born of pharaoh that sitteth upon his throne, even unto the first-born of the maid-servant that is behind the mill; and all the first-born of cattle.
english: and all the first-born in the land of Egypt shall die, from the first-born of Pharaoh that sitteth upon his throne, even unto the first-born of the maid-servant that is behind the mill; and all the first-born of cattle.

searchText: exodus 11:6 and there shall be a great cry throughout all the land of egypt, such as there hath been none like it, nor shall be like it any more.
english: And there shall be a great cry throughout all the land of Egypt, such as there hath been none like it, nor shall be like it any more.

searchText: exodus 11:7 but against any of the children of israel shall not a dog whet his tongue, against man or beast; that ye may know how that the lord doth put a difference between the egyptians and israel.
english: But against any of the children of Israel shall not a dog whet his tongue, against man or beast; that ye may know how that the LORD doth put a difference between the Egyptians and Israel.

searchText: exodus 11:8 and all these thy servants shall come down unto me, and bow down unto me, saying: get thee out, and all the people that follow thee; and after that i will go out.’ and he went out from pharaoh in hot anger.
english: And all these thy servants shall come down unto me, and bow down unto me, saying: Get thee out, and all the people that follow thee; and after that I will go out.’ And he went out from Pharaoh in hot anger.

searchText: exodus 11:9 and the lord said unto moses: ‘pharaoh will not hearken unto you; that my wonders may be multiplied in the land of egypt.’
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Pharaoh will not hearken unto you; that My wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.’

searchText: exodus 11:10 and moses and aaron did all these wonders before pharaoh; and the lord hardened pharaoh’s heart, and he did not let the children of israel go out of his land.
english: And Moses and Aaron did all these wonders before Pharaoh; and the LORD hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he did not let the children of Israel go out of his land.

searchText: exodus 12:1 and the lord spoke unto moses and aaron in the land of egypt, saying:
english: And the LORD spoke unto Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt, saying:

searchText: exodus 12:2 ’this month shall be unto you the beginning of months; it shall be the first month of the year to you.
english: ’This month shall be unto you the beginning of months; it shall be the first month of the year to you.

searchText: exodus 12:3 speak ye unto all the congregation of israel, saying: in the tenth day of this month they shall take to them every man a lamb, according to their fathers’houses, a lamb for a household;
english: Speak ye unto all the congregation of Israel, saying: In the tenth day of this month they shall take to them every man a lamb, according to their fathers’houses, a lamb for a household;

searchText: exodus 12:4 and if the household be too little for a lamb, then shall he and his neighbour next unto his house take one according to the number of the souls; according to every man’s eating ye shall make your count for the lamb.
english: and if the household be too little for a lamb, then shall he and his neighbour next unto his house take one according to the number of the souls; according to every man’s eating ye shall make your count for the lamb.

searchText: exodus 12:5 your lamb shall be without blemish, a male of the first year; ye shall take it from the sheep, or from the goats;
english: Your lamb shall be without blemish, a male of the first year; ye shall take it from the sheep, or from the goats;

searchText: exodus 12:6 and ye shall keep it unto the fourteenth day of the same month; and the whole assembly of the congregation of israel shall kill it at dusk.
english: and ye shall keep it unto the fourteenth day of the same month; and the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel shall kill it at dusk.

searchText: exodus 12:7 and they shall take of the blood, and put it on the two side-posts and on the lintel, upon the houses wherein they shall eat it.
english: And they shall take of the blood, and put it on the two side-posts and on the lintel, upon the houses wherein they shall eat it.

searchText: exodus 12:8 and they shall eat the flesh in that night, roast with fire, and unleavened bread; with bitter herbs they shall eat it.
english: And they shall eat the flesh in that night, roast with fire, and unleavened bread; with bitter herbs they shall eat it.

searchText: exodus 12:9 eat not of it raw, nor sodden at all with water, but roast with fire; its head with its legs and with the inwards thereof.
english: Eat not of it raw, nor sodden at all with water, but roast with fire; its head with its legs and with the inwards thereof.

searchText: exodus 12:10 and ye shall let nothing of it remain until the morning; but that which remaineth of it until the morning ye shall burn with fire.
english: And ye shall let nothing of it remain until the morning; but that which remaineth of it until the morning ye shall burn with fire.

searchText: exodus 12:11 and thus shall ye eat it: with your loins girded, your shoes on your feet, and your staff in your hand; and ye shall eat it in haste—it is the lord’s passover.
english: And thus shall ye eat it: with your loins girded, your shoes on your feet, and your staff in your hand; and ye shall eat it in haste—it is the LORD’s passover.

searchText: exodus 12:12 for i will go through the land of egypt in that night, and will smite all the first-born in the land of egypt, both man and beast; and against all the gods of egypt i will execute judgments: i am the lord.
english: For I will go through the land of Egypt in that night, and will smite all the first-born in the land of Egypt, both man and beast; and against all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgments: I am the LORD.

searchText: exodus 12:13 and the blood shall be to you for a token upon the houses where ye are; and when i see the blood, i will pass over you, and there shall no plague be upon you to destroy you, when i smite the land of egypt.
english: And the blood shall be to you for a token upon the houses where ye are; and when I see the blood, I will pass over you, and there shall no plague be upon you to destroy you, when I smite the land of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 12:14 and this day shall be unto you for a memorial, and ye shall keep it a feast to the lord; throughout your generations ye shall keep it a feast by an ordinance for ever.
english: And this day shall be unto you for a memorial, and ye shall keep it a feast to the LORD; throughout your generations ye shall keep it a feast by an ordinance for ever.

searchText: exodus 12:15 seven days shall ye eat unleavened bread; howbeit the first day ye shall put away leaven out of your houses; for whosoever eateth leavened bread from the first day until the seventh day, that soul shall be cut off from israel.
english: Seven days shall ye eat unleavened bread; howbeit the first day ye shall put away leaven out of your houses; for whosoever eateth leavened bread from the first day until the seventh day, that soul shall be cut off from Israel.

searchText: exodus 12:16 and in the first day there shall be to you a holy convocation, and in the seventh day a holy convocation; no manner of work shall be done in them, save that which every man must eat, that only may be done by you.
english: And in the first day there shall be to you a holy convocation, and in the seventh day a holy convocation; no manner of work shall be done in them, save that which every man must eat, that only may be done by you.

searchText: exodus 12:17 and ye shall observe the feast of unleavened bread; for in this selfsame day have i brought your hosts out of the land of egypt; therefore shall ye observe this day throughout your generations by an ordinance for ever.
english: And ye shall observe the feast of unleavened bread; for in this selfsame day have I brought your hosts out of the land of Egypt; therefore shall ye observe this day throughout your generations by an ordinance for ever.

searchText: exodus 12:18 in the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at even, ye shall eat unleavened bread, until the one and twentieth day of the month at even.
english: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at even, ye shall eat unleavened bread, until the one and twentieth day of the month at even.

searchText: exodus 12:19 seven days shall there be no leaven found in your houses; for whosoever eateth that which is leavened, that soul shall be cut off from the congregation of israel, whether he be a sojourner, or one that is born in the land.
english: Seven days shall there be no leaven found in your houses; for whosoever eateth that which is leavened, that soul shall be cut off from the congregation of Israel, whether he be a sojourner, or one that is born in the land.

searchText: exodus 12:20 ye shall eat nothing leavened; in all your habitations shall ye eat unleavened bread.’
english: Ye shall eat nothing leavened; in all your habitations shall ye eat unleavened bread.’

searchText: exodus 12:21 then moses called for all the elders of israel, and said unto them: ‘draw out, and take you lambs according to your families, and kill the passover lamb.
english: Then Moses called for all the elders of Israel, and said unto them: ‘Draw out, and take you lambs according to your families, and kill the passover lamb.

searchText: exodus 12:22 and ye shall take a bunch of hyssop, and dip it in the blood that is in the basin, and strike the lintel and the two side-posts with the blood that is in the basin; and none of you shall go out of the door of his house until the morning.
english: And ye shall take a bunch of hyssop, and dip it in the blood that is in the basin, and strike the lintel and the two side-posts with the blood that is in the basin; and none of you shall go out of the door of his house until the morning.

searchText: exodus 12:23 for the lord will pass through to smite the egyptians; and when he seeth the blood upon the lintel, and on the two side-posts, the lord will pass over the door, and will not suffer the destroyer to come in unto your houses to smite you.
english: For the LORD will pass through to smite the Egyptians; and when He seeth the blood upon the lintel, and on the two side-posts, the LORD will pass over the door, and will not suffer the destroyer to come in unto your houses to smite you.

searchText: exodus 12:24 and ye shall observe this thing for an ordinance to thee and to thy sons for ever.
english: And ye shall observe this thing for an ordinance to thee and to thy sons for ever.

searchText: exodus 12:25 and it shall come to pass, when ye be come to the land which the lord will give you, according as he hath promised, that ye shall keep this service.
english: And it shall come to pass, when ye be come to the land which the LORD will give you, according as He hath promised, that ye shall keep this service.

searchText: exodus 12:26 and it shall come to pass, when your children shall say unto you: what mean ye by this service?
english: And it shall come to pass, when your children shall say unto you: What mean ye by this service?

searchText: exodus 12:27 that ye shall say: it is the sacrifice of the lord’s passover, for that he passed over the houses of the children of israel in egypt, when he smote the egyptians, and delivered our houses.’ and the people bowed the head and worshipped.
english: that ye shall say: It is the sacrifice of the LORD’s passover, for that He passed over the houses of the children of Israel in Egypt, when He smote the Egyptians, and delivered our houses.’ And the people bowed the head and worshipped.

searchText: exodus 12:28 and the children of israel went and did so; as the lord had commanded moses and aaron, so did they.
english: And the children of Israel went and did so; as the LORD had commanded Moses and Aaron, so did they.

searchText: exodus 12:29 and it came to pass at midnight, that the lord smote all the firstborn in the land of egypt, from the first-born of pharaoh that sat on his throne unto the first-born of the captive that was in the dungeon; and all the first-born of cattle.
english: And it came to pass at midnight, that the LORD smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the first-born of Pharaoh that sat on his throne unto the first-born of the captive that was in the dungeon; and all the first-born of cattle.

searchText: exodus 12:30 and pharaoh rose up in the night, he, and all his servants, and all the egyptians; and there was a great cry in egypt; for there was not a house where there was not one dead.
english: And Pharaoh rose up in the night, he, and all his servants, and all the Egyptians; and there was a great cry in Egypt; for there was not a house where there was not one dead.

searchText: exodus 12:31 and he called for moses and aaron by night and said: ‘rise up, get you forth from among my people, both ye and the children of israel; and go, serve the lord, as ye have said.
english: And he called for Moses and Aaron by night and said: ‘Rise up, get you forth from among my people, both ye and the children of Israel; and go, serve the LORD, as ye have said.

searchText: exodus 12:32 take both your flocks and your herds, as ye have said, and be gone; and bless me also.’
english: Take both your flocks and your herds, as ye have said, and be gone; and bless me also.’

searchText: exodus 12:33 and the egyptians were urgent upon the people, to send them out of the land in haste; for they said: ‘we are all dead men.’
english: And the Egyptians were urgent upon the people, to send them out of the land in haste; for they said: ‘We are all dead men.’

searchText: exodus 12:34 and the people took their dough before it was leavened, their kneading-troughs being bound up in their clothes upon their shoulders.
english: And the people took their dough before it was leavened, their kneading-troughs being bound up in their clothes upon their shoulders.

searchText: exodus 12:35 and the children of israel did according to the word of moses; and they asked of the egyptians jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment.
english: And the children of Israel did according to the word of Moses; and they asked of the Egyptians jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment.

searchText: exodus 12:36 and the lord gave the people favour in the sight of the egyptians, so that they let them have what they asked. and they despoiled the egyptians.
english: And the LORD gave the people favour in the sight of the Egyptians, so that they let them have what they asked. And they despoiled the Egyptians.

searchText: exodus 12:37 and the children of israel journeyed from rameses to succoth, about six hundred thousand men on foot, beside children.
english: And the children of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand men on foot, beside children.

searchText: exodus 12:38 and a mixed multitude went up also with them; and flocks, and herds, even very much cattle.
english: And a mixed multitude went up also with them; and flocks, and herds, even very much cattle.

searchText: exodus 12:39 and they baked unleavened cakes of the dough which they brought forth out of egypt, for it was not leavened; because they were thrust out of egypt, and could not tarry, neither had they prepared for themselves any victual.
english: And they baked unleavened cakes of the dough which they brought forth out of Egypt, for it was not leavened; because they were thrust out of Egypt, and could not tarry, neither had they prepared for themselves any victual.

searchText: exodus 12:40 now the time that the children of israel dwelt in egypt was four hundred and thirty years.
english: Now the time that the children of Israel dwelt in Egypt was four hundred and thirty years.

searchText: exodus 12:41 and it came to pass at the end of four hundred and thirty years, even the selfsame day it came to pass, that all the host of the lord went out from the land of egypt.
english: And it came to pass at the end of four hundred and thirty years, even the selfsame day it came to pass, that all the host of the LORD went out from the land of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 12:42 it was a night of watching unto the lord for bringing them out from the land of egypt; this same night is a night of watching unto the lord for all the children of israel throughout their generations.
english: It was a night of watching unto the LORD for bringing them out from the land of Egypt; this same night is a night of watching unto the LORD for all the children of Israel throughout their generations.

searchText: exodus 12:43 and the lord said unto moses and aaron: ‘this is the ordinance of the passover: there shall no alien eat thereof;
english: And the LORD said unto Moses and Aaron: ‘This is the ordinance of the passover: there shall no alien eat thereof;

searchText: exodus 12:44 but every man’s servant that is bought for money, when thou hast circumcised him, then shall he eat thereof.
english: but every man’s servant that is bought for money, when thou hast circumcised him, then shall he eat thereof.

searchText: exodus 12:45 a sojourner and a hired servant shall not eat thereof.
english: A sojourner and a hired servant shall not eat thereof.

searchText: exodus 12:46 in one house shall it be eaten; thou shalt not carry forth aught of the flesh abroad out of the house; neither shall ye break a bone thereof.
english: In one house shall it be eaten; thou shalt not carry forth aught of the flesh abroad out of the house; neither shall ye break a bone thereof.

searchText: exodus 12:47 all the congregation of israel shall keep it.
english: All the congregation of Israel shall keep it.

searchText: exodus 12:48 and when a stranger shall sojourn with thee, and will keep the passover to the lord, let all his males be circumcised, and then let him come near and keep it; and he shall be as one that is born in the land; but no uncircumcised person shall eat thereof.
english: And when a stranger shall sojourn with thee, and will keep the passover to the LORD, let all his males be circumcised, and then let him come near and keep it; and he shall be as one that is born in the land; but no uncircumcised person shall eat thereof.
