searchText: exodus 12:49 one law shall be to him that is homeborn, and unto the stranger that sojourneth among you.’
english: One law shall be to him that is homeborn, and unto the stranger that sojourneth among you.’

searchText: exodus 12:50 thus did all the children of israel; as the lord commanded moses and aaron, so did they.
english: Thus did all the children of Israel; as the LORD commanded Moses and Aaron, so did they.

searchText: exodus 12:51 and it came to pass the selfsame day that the lord did bring the children of israel out of the land of egypt by their hosts.
english: And it came to pass the selfsame day that the LORD did bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt by their hosts.

searchText: exodus 13:1 and the lord spoke unto moses, saying:
english: And the LORD spoke unto Moses, saying:

searchText: exodus 13:2 ’sanctify unto me all the first-born, whatsoever opens the womb among the children of israel, both of man and of beast, it is mine.’
english: ’Sanctify unto Me all the first-born, whatsoever opens the womb among the children of Israel, both of man and of beast, it is Mine.’

searchText: exodus 13:3 and moses said unto the people: ‘remember this day, in which ye came out from egypt, out of the house of bondage; for by strength of hand the lord brought you out from this place; there shall no leavened bread be eaten.
english: And Moses said unto the people: ‘Remember this day, in which ye came out from Egypt, out of the house of bondage; for by strength of hand the LORD brought you out from this place; there shall no leavened bread be eaten.

searchText: exodus 13:4 this day ye go forth in the month abib.
english: This day ye go forth in the month Abib.

searchText: exodus 13:5 and it shall be when the lord shall bring thee into the land of the canaanite, and the hittite, and the amorite, and the hivite, and the jebusite, which he swore unto thy fathers to give thee, a land flowing with milk and honey, that thou shalt keep this service in this month.
english: And it shall be when the LORD shall bring thee into the land of the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Amorite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite, which He swore unto thy fathers to give thee, a land flowing with milk and honey, that thou shalt keep this service in this month.

searchText: exodus 13:6 seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, and in the seventh day shall be a feast to the lord.
english: Seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, and in the seventh day shall be a feast to the LORD.

searchText: exodus 13:7 unleavened bread shall be eaten throughout the seven days; and there shall no leavened bread be seen with thee, neither shall there be leaven seen with thee, in all thy borders.
english: Unleavened bread shall be eaten throughout the seven days; and there shall no leavened bread be seen with thee, neither shall there be leaven seen with thee, in all thy borders.

searchText: exodus 13:8 and thou shalt tell thy son in that day, saying: it is because of that which the lord did for me when i came forth out of egypt.
english: And thou shalt tell thy son in that day, saying: It is because of that which the LORD did for me when I came forth out of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 13:9 and it shall be for a sign unto thee upon thy hand, and for a memorial between thine eyes, that the law of the lord may be in thy mouth; for with a strong hand hath the lord brought thee out of egypt.
english: And it shall be for a sign unto thee upon thy hand, and for a memorial between thine eyes, that the law of the LORD may be in thy mouth; for with a strong hand hath the LORD brought thee out of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 13:10 thou shalt therefore keep this ordinance in its season from year to year.
english: Thou shalt therefore keep this ordinance in its season from year to year.

searchText: exodus 13:11 and it shall be when the lord shall bring thee into the land of the canaanite, as he swore unto thee and to thy fathers, and shall give it thee,
english: And it shall be when the LORD shall bring thee into the land of the Canaanite, as He swore unto thee and to thy fathers, and shall give it thee,

searchText: exodus 13:12 that thou shalt set apart unto the lord all that openeth the womb; every firstling that is a male, which thou hast coming of a beast, shall be the lord’s.
english: that thou shalt set apart unto the LORD all that openeth the womb; every firstling that is a male, which thou hast coming of a beast, shall be the LORD’s.

searchText: exodus 13:13 and every firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a lamb; and if thou wilt not redeem it, then thou shalt break its neck; and all the first-born of man among thy sons shalt thou redeem.
english: And every firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a lamb; and if thou wilt not redeem it, then thou shalt break its neck; and all the first-born of man among thy sons shalt thou redeem.

searchText: exodus 13:14 and it shall be when thy son asketh thee in time to come, saying: what is this? that thou shalt say unto him: by strength of hand the lord brought us out from egypt, from the house of bondage;
english: And it shall be when thy son asketh thee in time to come, saying: What is this? that thou shalt say unto him: By strength of hand the LORD brought us out from Egypt, from the house of bondage;

searchText: exodus 13:15 and it came to pass, when pharaoh would hardly let us go that the lord slew all the firstborn in the land of egypt, both the first-born of man, and the first-born of beast; therefore i sacrifice to the lord all that openeth the womb, being males; but all the first-born of my sons i redeem.
english: and it came to pass, when Pharaoh would hardly let us go that the LORD slew all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both the first-born of man, and the first-born of beast; therefore I sacrifice to the LORD all that openeth the womb, being males; but all the first-born of my sons I redeem.

searchText: exodus 13:16 and it shall be for a sign upon thy hand, and for frontlets between your eyes; for by strength of hand the lord brought us forth out of egypt.’
english: And it shall be for a sign upon thy hand, and for frontlets between your eyes; for by strength of hand the LORD brought us forth out of Egypt.’

searchText: exodus 13:17 and it came to pass, when pharaoh had let the people go, that god led them not by the way of the land of the philistines, although that was near; for god said: 'lest the people regret when they see war, and they return to egypt.’
english: And it came to pass, when Pharaoh had let the people go, that God led them not by the way of the land of the Philistines, although that was near; for God said: 'Lest the people regret when they see war, and they return to Egypt.’

searchText: exodus 13:18 but god led the people about, by the way of the wilderness by the red sea; and the children of israel went up armed out of the land of egypt.
english: But God led the people about, by the way of the wilderness by the Red Sea; and the children of Israel went up armed out of the land of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 13:19 and moses took the bones of joseph with him; for he had surely sworn the children of israel, saying: ‘god will surely remember you; and ye shall carry up my bones away hence with you.’
english: And Moses took the bones of Joseph with him; for he had surely sworn the children of Israel, saying: ‘God will surely remember you; and ye shall carry up my bones away hence with you.’

searchText: exodus 13:20 and they took their journey from succoth, and encamped in etham, in the edge of the wilderness.
english: And they took their journey from Succoth, and encamped in Etham, in the edge of the wilderness.

searchText: exodus 13:21 and the lord went before them by day in a pillar of cloud, to lead them the way; and by night in a pillar of fire, to give them light; that they might go by day and by night:
english: And the LORD went before them by day in a pillar of cloud, to lead them the way; and by night in a pillar of fire, to give them light; that they might go by day and by night:

searchText: exodus 13:22 the pillar of cloud by day, and the pillar of fire by night, departed not from before the people.
english: the pillar of cloud by day, and the pillar of fire by night, departed not from before the people.

searchText: exodus 14:1 and the lord spoke unto moses, saying:
english: And the LORD spoke unto Moses, saying:

searchText: exodus 14:2 ’speak unto the children of israel, that they turn back and encamp before pi-hahiroth, between migdol and the sea, before baal-zephon, over against it shall ye encamp by the sea.
english: ’Speak unto the children of Israel, that they turn back and encamp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, before Baal-zephon, over against it shall ye encamp by the sea.

searchText: exodus 14:3 and pharaoh will say of the children of israel: they are entangled in the land, the wilderness hath shut them in.
english: And Pharaoh will say of the children of Israel: They are entangled in the land, the wilderness hath shut them in.

searchText: exodus 14:4 and i will harden pharaoh’s heart, and he shall follow after them; and i will get me honour upon pharaoh, and upon all his host; and the egyptians shall know that i am the lord.’ and they did so.
english: And I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, and he shall follow after them; and I will get Me honour upon Pharaoh, and upon all his host; and the Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD.’ And they did so.

searchText: exodus 14:5 and it was told the king of egypt that the people were fled; and the heart of pharaoh and of his servants was turned towards the people, and they said: ‘what is this we have done, that we have let israel go from serving us?
english: And it was told the king of Egypt that the people were fled; and the heart of Pharaoh and of his servants was turned towards the people, and they said: ‘What is this we have done, that we have let Israel go from serving us?

searchText: exodus 14:6 and he made ready his chariots, and took his people with him.
english: And he made ready his chariots, and took his people with him.

searchText: exodus 14:7 and he took six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots of egypt, and captains over all of them.
english: And he took six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots of Egypt, and captains over all of them.

searchText: exodus 14:8 and the lord hardened the heart of pharaoh king of egypt, and he pursued after the children of israel; for the children of israel went out with a high hand.
english: And the LORD hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he pursued after the children of Israel; for the children of Israel went out with a high hand.

searchText: exodus 14:9 and the egyptians pursued after them, all the horses and chariots of pharaoh, and his horsemen, and his army, and overtook them encamping by the sea, beside pi-hahiroth, in front of baal-zephon.
english: And the Egyptians pursued after them, all the horses and chariots of Pharaoh, and his horsemen, and his army, and overtook them encamping by the sea, beside Pi-hahiroth, in front of Baal-zephon.

searchText: exodus 14:10 and when pharaoh drew nigh, the children of israel lifted up their eyes, and, behold, the egyptians were marching after them; and they were sore afraid; and the children of israel cried out unto the lord.
english: And when Pharaoh drew nigh, the children of Israel lifted up their eyes, and, behold, the Egyptians were marching after them; and they were sore afraid; and the children of Israel cried out unto the LORD.

searchText: exodus 14:11 and they said unto moses: ‘because there were no graves in egypt, hast thou taken us away to die in the wilderness? wherefore hast thou dealt thus with us, to bring us forth out of egypt?
english: And they said unto Moses: ‘Because there were no graves in Egypt, hast thou taken us away to die in the wilderness? wherefore hast thou dealt thus with us, to bring us forth out of Egypt?

searchText: exodus 14:12 is not this the word that we spoke unto thee in egypt, saying: let us alone, that we may serve the egyptians? for it were better for us to serve the egyptians, than that we should die in the wilderness.’
english: Is not this the word that we spoke unto thee in Egypt, saying: Let us alone, that we may serve the Egyptians? For it were better for us to serve the Egyptians, than that we should die in the wilderness.’

searchText: exodus 14:13 and moses said unto the people: ‘fear ye not, stand still, and see the salvation of the lord, which he will work for you to-day; for whereas ye have seen the egyptians to-day, ye shall see them again no more for ever.
english: And Moses said unto the people: ‘Fear ye not, stand still, and see the salvation of the LORD, which He will work for you to-day; for whereas ye have seen the Egyptians to-day, ye shall see them again no more for ever.

searchText: exodus 14:14 the lord will fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.’
english: The LORD will fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.’

searchText: exodus 14:15 and the lord said unto moses: ‘wherefore criest thou unto me? speak unto the children of israel, that they go forward.
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Wherefore criest thou unto Me? speak unto the children of Israel, that they go forward.

searchText: exodus 14:16 and lift thou up thy rod, and stretch out thy hand over the sea, and divide it; and the children of israel shall go into the midst of the sea on dry ground.
english: And lift thou up thy rod, and stretch out thy hand over the sea, and divide it; and the children of Israel shall go into the midst of the sea on dry ground.

searchText: exodus 14:17 and i, behold, i will harden the hearts of the egyptians, and they shall go in after them; and i will get me honour upon pharaoh, and upon all his host, upon his chariots, and upon his horsemen.
english: And I, behold, I will harden the hearts of the Egyptians, and they shall go in after them; and I will get Me honour upon Pharaoh, and upon all his host, upon his chariots, and upon his horsemen.

searchText: exodus 14:18 and the egyptians shall know that i am the lord, when i have gotten me honour upon pharaoh, upon his chariots, and upon his horsemen.’
english: And the Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD, when I have gotten Me honour upon Pharaoh, upon his chariots, and upon his horsemen.’

searchText: exodus 14:19 and the angel of god, who went before the camp of israel, removed and went behind them; and the pillar of cloud removed from before them, and stood behind them;
english: And the angel of God, who went before the camp of Israel, removed and went behind them; and the pillar of cloud removed from before them, and stood behind them;

searchText: exodus 14:20 and it came between the camp of egypt and the camp of israel; and there was the cloud and the darkness here, yet gave it light by night there; and the one came not near the other all the night.
english: and it came between the camp of Egypt and the camp of Israel; and there was the cloud and the darkness here, yet gave it light by night there; and the one came not near the other all the night.

searchText: exodus 14:21 and moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and the lord caused the sea to go back by a strong east wind all the night, and made the sea dry land, and the waters were divided.
english: And Moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and the LORD caused the sea to go back by a strong east wind all the night, and made the sea dry land, and the waters were divided.

searchText: exodus 14:22 and the children of israel went into the midst of the sea upon the dry ground; and the waters were a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left.
english: And the children of Israel went into the midst of the sea upon the dry ground; and the waters were a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left.

searchText: exodus 14:23 and the egyptians pursued, and went in after them into the midst of the sea, all pharaoh’s horses, his chariots, and his horsemen.
english: And the Egyptians pursued, and went in after them into the midst of the sea, all Pharaoh’s horses, his chariots, and his horsemen.

searchText: exodus 14:24 and it came to pass in the morning watch, that the lord looked forth upon the host of the egyptians through the pillar of fire and of cloud, and discomfited the host of the egyptians.
english: And it came to pass in the morning watch, that the LORD looked forth upon the host of the Egyptians through the pillar of fire and of cloud, and discomfited the host of the Egyptians.

searchText: exodus 14:25 and he took off their chariot wheels, and made them to drive heavily; so that the egyptians said: ‘let us flee from the face of israel; for the lord fighteth for them against the egyptians.’
english: And He took off their chariot wheels, and made them to drive heavily; so that the Egyptians said: ‘Let us flee from the face of Israel; for the LORD fighteth for them against the Egyptians.’

searchText: exodus 14:26 and the lord said unto moses: ‘stretch out thy hand over the sea, that the waters may come back upon the egyptians, upon their chariots, and upon their horsemen.’
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Stretch out thy hand over the sea, that the waters may come back upon the Egyptians, upon their chariots, and upon their horsemen.’

searchText: exodus 14:27 and moses stretched forth his hand over the sea, and the sea returned to its strength when the morning appeared; and the egyptians fled against it; and the lord overthrew the egyptians in the midst of the sea.
english: And Moses stretched forth his hand over the sea, and the sea returned to its strength when the morning appeared; and the Egyptians fled against it; and the LORD overthrew the Egyptians in the midst of the sea.

searchText: exodus 14:28 and the waters returned, and covered the chariots, and the horsemen, even all the host of pharaoh that went in after them into the sea; there remained not so much as one of them.
english: And the waters returned, and covered the chariots, and the horsemen, even all the host of Pharaoh that went in after them into the sea; there remained not so much as one of them.

searchText: exodus 14:29 but the children of israel walked upon dry land in the midst of the sea; and the waters were a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left.
english: But the children of Israel walked upon dry land in the midst of the sea; and the waters were a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left.

searchText: exodus 14:30 thus the lord saved israel that day out of the hand of the egyptians; and israel saw the egyptians dead upon the sea-shore.
english: Thus the LORD saved Israel that day out of the hand of the Egyptians; and Israel saw the Egyptians dead upon the sea-shore.

searchText: exodus 14:31 and israel saw the great work which the lord did upon the egyptians, and the people feared the lord; and they believed in the lord, and in his servant moses.
english: And Israel saw the great work which the LORD did upon the Egyptians, and the people feared the LORD; and they believed in the LORD, and in His servant Moses.

searchText: exodus 15:1 then sang moses and the children of israel this song unto the lord, and spoke, saying: i will sing unto the lord, for he is highly exalted; the horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.
english: Then sang Moses and the children of Israel this song unto the LORD, and spoke, saying: I will sing unto the LORD, for He is highly exalted; The horse and his rider hath He thrown into the sea.

searchText: exodus 15:2 the lord is my strength and song, and he is become my salvation; this is my god, and i will glorify him; my father’s god, and i will exalt him.
english: The LORD is my strength and song, And He is become my salvation; This is my God, and I will glorify Him; My father’s God, and I will exalt Him.

searchText: exodus 15:3 the lord is a man of war, the lord is his name.
english: The LORD is a man of war, The LORD is His name.

searchText: exodus 15:4 pharaoh’s chariots and his host hath he cast into the sea, and his chosen captains are sunk in the red sea.
english: Pharaoh’s chariots and his host hath He cast into the sea, And his chosen captains are sunk in the Red Sea.

searchText: exodus 15:5 the deeps cover them— they went down into the depths like a stone.
english: The deeps cover them— They went down into the depths like a stone.

searchText: exodus 15:6 thy right hand, o lord, glorious in power, thy right hand, o lord, dasheth in pieces the enemy.
english: Thy right hand, O LORD, glorious in power, Thy right hand, O LORD, dasheth in pieces the enemy.

searchText: exodus 15:7 and in the greatness of thine excellency thou overthrowest them that rise up against thee; thou sendest forth thy wrath, it consumeth them as stubble.
english: And in the greatness of Thine excellency Thou overthrowest them that rise up against Thee; Thou sendest forth Thy wrath, it consumeth them as stubble.

searchText: exodus 15:8 and with the blast of thy nostrils the waters were piled up— the floods stood upright as a heap; the deeps were congealed in the heart of the sea.
english: And with the blast of Thy nostrils the waters were piled up— The floods stood upright as a heap; The deeps were congealed in the heart of the sea.

searchText: exodus 15:9 the enemy said: ‘i will pursue, i will overtake, i will divide the spoil; my lust shall be satisfied upon them; i will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them.’
english: The enemy said: ‘I will pursue, I will overtake, I will divide the spoil; My lust shall be satisfied upon them; I will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them.’

searchText: exodus 15:10 thou didst blow with thy wind, the sea covered them; they sank as lead in the mighty waters.
english: Thou didst blow with Thy wind, the sea covered them; They sank as lead in the mighty waters.

searchText: exodus 15:11 who is like unto thee, o lord, among the mighty? who is like unto thee, glorious in holiness, fearful in praises, doing wonders?
english: Who is like unto Thee, O LORD, among the mighty? Who is like unto Thee, glorious in holiness, Fearful in praises, doing wonders?

searchText: exodus 15:12 thou stretchedst out thy right hand— the earth swallowed them.
english: Thou stretchedst out Thy right hand— The earth swallowed them.

searchText: exodus 15:13 thou in thy love hast led the people that thou hast redeemed; thou hast guided them in thy strength to thy holy habitation.
english: Thou in Thy love hast led the people that Thou hast redeemed; Thou hast guided them in Thy strength to Thy holy habitation.

searchText: exodus 15:14 the peoples have heard, they tremble; pangs have taken hold on the inhabitants of philistia.
english: The peoples have heard, they tremble; Pangs have taken hold on the inhabitants of Philistia.

searchText: exodus 15:15 then were the chiefs of edom affrighted; the mighty men of moab, trembling taketh hold upon them; all the inhabitants of canaan are melted away.
english: Then were the chiefs of Edom affrighted; The mighty men of Moab, trembling taketh hold upon them; All the inhabitants of Canaan are melted away.

searchText: exodus 15:16 terror and dread falleth upon them; by the greatness of thine arm they are as still as a stone; till thy people pass over, o lord, till the people pass over that thou hast gotten.
english: Terror and dread falleth upon them; By the greatness of Thine arm they are as still as a stone; Till Thy people pass over, O LORD, Till the people pass over that Thou hast gotten.

searchText: exodus 15:17 thou bringest them in, and plantest them in the mountain of thine inheritance, the place, o lord, which thou hast made for thee to dwell in, the sanctuary, o lord, which thy hands have established.
english: Thou bringest them in, and plantest them in the mountain of Thine inheritance, The place, O LORD, which Thou hast made for Thee to dwell in, The sanctuary, O Lord, which Thy hands have established.

searchText: exodus 15:18 the lord shall reign for ever and ever.
english: The LORD shall reign for ever and ever.

searchText: exodus 15:19 for the horses of pharaoh went in with his chariots and with his horsemen into the sea, and the lord brought back the waters of the sea upon them; but the children of israel walked on dry land in the midst of the sea.
english: For the horses of Pharaoh went in with his chariots and with his horsemen into the sea, and the LORD brought back the waters of the sea upon them; but the children of Israel walked on dry land in the midst of the sea.

searchText: exodus 15:20 and miriam the prophetess, the sister of aaron, took a timbrel in her hand; and all the women went out after her with timbrels and with dances.
english: And Miriam the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took a timbrel in her hand; and all the women went out after her with timbrels and with dances.

searchText: exodus 15:21 and miriam sang unto them: sing ye to the lord, for he is highly exalted: the horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.
english: And Miriam sang unto them: Sing ye to the LORD, for He is highly exalted: The horse and his rider hath He thrown into the sea.

searchText: exodus 15:22 and moses led israel onward from the red sea, and they went out into the wilderness of shur; and they went three days in the wilderness, and found no water.
english: And Moses led Israel onward from the Red Sea, and they went out into the wilderness of Shur; and they went three days in the wilderness, and found no water.

searchText: exodus 15:23 and when they came to marah, they could not drink of the waters of marah, for they were bitter. therefore the name of it was called marah.
english: And when they came to Marah, they could not drink of the waters of Marah, for they were bitter. Therefore the name of it was called Marah.

searchText: exodus 15:24 and the people murmured against moses, saying: ‘what shall we drink?’
english: And the people murmured against Moses, saying: ‘What shall we drink?’

searchText: exodus 15:25 and he cried unto the lord; and the lord showed him a tree, and he cast it into the waters, and the waters were made sweet. there he made for them a statute and an ordinance, and there he proved them;
english: And he cried unto the LORD; and the LORD showed him a tree, and he cast it into the waters, and the waters were made sweet. There He made for them a statute and an ordinance, and there He proved them;

searchText: exodus 15:26 and he said: ‘if thou wilt diligently hearken to the voice of the lord thy god, and wilt do that which is right in his eyes, and wilt give ear to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, i will put none of the diseases upon thee, which i have put upon the egyptians; for i am the lord that healeth thee.’
english: and He said: ‘If thou wilt diligently hearken to the voice of the LORD thy God, and wilt do that which is right in His eyes, and wilt give ear to His commandments, and keep all His statutes, I will put none of the diseases upon thee, which I have put upon the Egyptians; for I am the LORD that healeth thee.’

searchText: exodus 15:27 and they came to elim, where were twelve springs of water, and three score and ten palm-trees; and they encamped there by the waters.
english: And they came to Elim, where were twelve springs of water, and three score and ten palm-trees; and they encamped there by the waters.

searchText: exodus 16:1 and they took their journey from elim, and all the congregation of the children of israel came unto the wilderness of sin, which is between elim and sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after their departing out of the land of egypt.
english: And they took their journey from Elim, and all the congregation of the children of Israel came unto the wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after their departing out of the land of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 16:2 and the whole congregation of the children of israel murmured against moses and against aaron in the wilderness;
english: And the whole congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron in the wilderness;

searchText: exodus 16:3 and the children of israel said unto them: ‘would that we had died by the hand of the lord in the land of egypt, when we sat by the flesh-pots, when we did eat bread to the full; for ye have brought us forth into this wilderness, to kill this whole assembly with hunger.’
english: and the children of Israel said unto them: ‘Would that we had died by the hand of the LORD in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the flesh-pots, when we did eat bread to the full; for ye have brought us forth into this wilderness, to kill this whole assembly with hunger.’

searchText: exodus 16:4 then said the lord unto moses: ‘behold, i will cause to rain bread from heaven for you; and the people shall go out and gather a day’s portion every day, that i may prove them, whether they will walk in my law, or not.
english: Then said the LORD unto Moses: ‘Behold, I will cause to rain bread from heaven for you; and the people shall go out and gather a day’s portion every day, that I may prove them, whether they will walk in My law, or not.

searchText: exodus 16:5 and it shall come to pass on the sixth day that they shall prepare that which they bring in, and it shall be twice as much as they gather daily.’
english: And it shall come to pass on the sixth day that they shall prepare that which they bring in, and it shall be twice as much as they gather daily.’

searchText: exodus 16:6 and moses and aaron said unto all the children of israel: ‘at even, then ye shall know that the lord hath brought you out from the land of egypt;
english: And Moses and Aaron said unto all the children of Israel: ‘At even, then ye shall know that the LORD hath brought you out from the land of Egypt;

searchText: exodus 16:7 and in the morning, then ye shall see the glory of the lord; for that he hath heard your murmurings against the lord; and what are we, that ye murmur against us?’
english: and in the morning, then ye shall see the glory of the LORD; for that He hath heard your murmurings against the LORD; and what are we, that ye murmur against us?’

searchText: exodus 16:8 and moses said: ‘this shall be, when the lord shall give you in the evening flesh to eat, and in the morning bread to the full; for that the lord heareth your murmurings which ye murmur against him; and what are we? your murmurings are not against us, but against the lord.’
english: And Moses said: ‘This shall be, when the LORD shall give you in the evening flesh to eat, and in the morning bread to the full; for that the LORD heareth your murmurings which ye murmur against Him; and what are we? your murmurings are not against us, but against the LORD.’

searchText: exodus 16:9 and moses said unto aaron: ‘say unto all the congregation of the children of israel: come near before the lord; for he hath heard your murmurings.’
english: And Moses said unto Aaron: ‘Say unto all the congregation of the children of Israel: Come near before the LORD; for He hath heard your murmurings.’

searchText: exodus 16:10 and it came to pass, as aaron spoke unto the whole congregation of the children of israel, that they looked toward the wilderness, and, behold, the glory of the lord appeared in the cloud.
english: And it came to pass, as Aaron spoke unto the whole congregation of the children of Israel, that they looked toward the wilderness, and, behold, the glory of the LORD appeared in the cloud.

searchText: exodus 16:11 and the lord spoke unto moses, saying:
english: And the LORD spoke unto Moses, saying:

searchText: exodus 16:12 ’i have heard the murmurings of the children of israel. speak unto them, saying: at dusk ye shall eat flesh, and in the morning ye shall be filled with bread; and ye shall know that i am the lord your god.’
english: ’I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel. Speak unto them, saying: At dusk ye shall eat flesh, and in the morning ye shall be filled with bread; and ye shall know that I am the LORD your God.’

searchText: exodus 16:13 and it came to pass at even, that the quails came up, and covered the camp; and in the morning there was a layer of dew round about the camp.
english: And it came to pass at even, that the quails came up, and covered the camp; and in the morning there was a layer of dew round about the camp.

searchText: exodus 16:14 and when the layer of dew was gone up, behold upon the face of the wilderness a fine, scale-like thing, fine as the hoar-frost on the ground.
english: And when the layer of dew was gone up, behold upon the face of the wilderness a fine, scale-like thing, fine as the hoar-frost on the ground.

searchText: exodus 16:15 and when the children of israel saw it, they said one to another: ‘what is it?’—for they knew not what it was. and moses said unto them: ‘it is the bread which the lord hath given you to eat.
english: And when the children of Israel saw it, they said one to another: ‘What is it?’—for they knew not what it was. And Moses said unto them: ‘It is the bread which the LORD hath given you to eat.

searchText: exodus 16:16 this is the thing which the lord hath commanded: gather ye of it every man according to his eating; an omer a head, according to the number of your persons, shall ye take it, every man for them that are in his tent.’
english: This is the thing which the LORD hath commanded: Gather ye of it every man according to his eating; an omer a head, according to the number of your persons, shall ye take it, every man for them that are in his tent.’

searchText: exodus 16:17 and the children of israel did so, and gathered some more, some less.
english: And the children of Israel did so, and gathered some more, some less.

searchText: exodus 16:18 and when they did mete it with an omer, he that gathered much had nothing over, and he that gathered little had no lack; they gathered every man according to his eating.
english: And when they did mete it with an omer, he that gathered much had nothing over, and he that gathered little had no lack; they gathered every man according to his eating.

searchText: exodus 16:19 and moses said unto them: ‘let no man leave of it till the morning.’
english: And Moses said unto them: ‘Let no man leave of it till the morning.’

searchText: exodus 16:20 notwithstanding they hearkened not unto moses; but some of them left of it until the morning, and it bred worms, and rotted; and moses was wroth with them.
english: Notwithstanding they hearkened not unto Moses; but some of them left of it until the morning, and it bred worms, and rotted; and Moses was wroth with them.

searchText: exodus 16:21 and they gathered it morning by morning, every man according to his eating; and as the sun waxed hot, it melted.
english: And they gathered it morning by morning, every man according to his eating; and as the sun waxed hot, it melted.

searchText: exodus 16:22 and it came to pass that on the sixth day they gathered twice as much bread, two omers for each one; and all the rulers of the congregation came and told moses.
english: And it came to pass that on the sixth day they gathered twice as much bread, two omers for each one; and all the rulers of the congregation came and told Moses.

searchText: exodus 16:23 and he said unto them: ‘this is that which the lord hath spoken: to-morrow is a solemn rest, a holy sabbath unto the lord. bake that which ye will bake, and seethe that which ye will seethe; and all that remaineth over lay up for you to be kept until the morning.’
english: And he said unto them: ‘This is that which the LORD hath spoken: To-morrow is a solemn rest, a holy sabbath unto the LORD. Bake that which ye will bake, and seethe that which ye will seethe; and all that remaineth over lay up for you to be kept until the morning.’

searchText: exodus 16:24 and they laid it up till the morning, as moses bade; and it did not rot, neither was there any worm therein.
english: And they laid it up till the morning, as Moses bade; and it did not rot, neither was there any worm therein.

searchText: exodus 16:25 and moses said: ‘eat that to-day; for to-day is a sabbath unto the lord; to-day ye shall not find it in the field.
english: And Moses said: ‘Eat that to-day; for to-day is a sabbath unto the LORD; to-day ye shall not find it in the field.

searchText: exodus 16:26 six days ye shall gather it; but on the seventh day is the sabbath, in it there shall be none.’
english: Six days ye shall gather it; but on the seventh day is the sabbath, in it there shall be none.’

searchText: exodus 16:27 and it came to pass on the seventh day, that there went out some of the people to gather, and they found none.
english: And it came to pass on the seventh day, that there went out some of the people to gather, and they found none.

searchText: exodus 16:28 and the lord said unto moses: ‘how long refuse ye to keep my commandments and my laws?
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘How long refuse ye to keep My commandments and My laws?

searchText: exodus 16:29 see that the lord hath given you the sabbath; therefore he giveth you on the sixth day the bread of two days; abide ye every man in his place, let no man go out of his place on the seventh day.’
english: See that the LORD hath given you the sabbath; therefore He giveth you on the sixth day the bread of two days; abide ye every man in his place, let no man go out of his place on the seventh day.’

searchText: exodus 16:30 so the people rested on the seventh day.
english: So the people rested on the seventh day.

searchText: exodus 16:31 and the house of israel called the name thereof manna; and it was like coriander seed, white; and the taste of it was like wafers made with honey.
english: And the house of Israel called the name thereof Manna; and it was like coriander seed, white; and the taste of it was like wafers made with honey.

searchText: exodus 16:32 and moses said: ‘this is the thing which the lord hath commanded: let an omerful of it be kept throughout your generations; that they may see the bread wherewith i fed you in the wilderness, when i brought you forth from the land of egypt.’
english: And Moses said: ‘This is the thing which the LORD hath commanded: Let an omerful of it be kept throughout your generations; that they may see the bread wherewith I fed you in the wilderness, when I brought you forth from the land of Egypt.’

searchText: exodus 16:33 and moses said unto aaron: ‘take a jar, and put an omerful of manna therein, and lay it up before the lord, to be kept throughout your generations.’
english: And Moses said unto Aaron: ‘Take a jar, and put an omerful of manna therein, and lay it up before the LORD, to be kept throughout your generations.’

searchText: exodus 16:34 as the lord commanded moses, so aaron laid it up before the testimony, to be kept.
english: As the LORD commanded Moses, so Aaron laid it up before the Testimony, to be kept.

searchText: exodus 16:35 and the children of israel did eat the manna forty years, until they came to a land inhabited; they did eat the manna, until they came unto the borders of the land of canaan.
english: And the children of Israel did eat the manna forty years, until they came to a land inhabited; they did eat the manna, until they came unto the borders of the land of Canaan.

searchText: exodus 16:36 now an omer is the tenth part of an ephah.
english: Now an omer is the tenth part of an ephah.

searchText: exodus 17:1 and all the congregation of the children of israel journeyed from the wilderness of sin, by their stages, according to the commandment of the lord, and encamped in rephidim; and there was no water for the people to drink.
english: And all the congregation of the children of Israel journeyed from the wilderness of Sin, by their stages, according to the commandment of the LORD, and encamped in Rephidim; and there was no water for the people to drink.

searchText: exodus 17:2 wherefore the people strove with moses, and said: ‘give us water that we may drink.’ and moses said unto them: ‘why strive ye with me? wherefore do ye try the lord?’
english: Wherefore the people strove with Moses, and said: ‘Give us water that we may drink.’ And Moses said unto them: ‘Why strive ye with me? wherefore do ye try the LORD?’

searchText: exodus 17:3 and the people thirsted there for water; and the people murmured against moses, and said: ‘wherefore hast thou brought us up out of egypt, to kill us and our children and our cattle with thirst?’
english: And the people thirsted there for water; and the people murmured against Moses, and said: ‘Wherefore hast thou brought us up out of Egypt, to kill us and our children and our cattle with thirst?’

searchText: exodus 17:4 and moses cried unto the lord, saying: ‘what shall i do unto this people? they are almost ready to stone me.’
english: And Moses cried unto the LORD, saying: ‘What shall I do unto this people? they are almost ready to stone me.’

searchText: exodus 17:5 and the lord said unto moses: ‘pass on before the people, and take with thee of the elders of israel; and thy rod, wherewith thou smotest the river, take in thy hand, and go.
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Pass on before the people, and take with thee of the elders of Israel; and thy rod, wherewith thou smotest the river, take in thy hand, and go.

searchText: exodus 17:6 behold, i will stand before thee there upon the rock in horeb; and thou shalt smite the rock, and there shall come water out of it, that the people may drink.’ and moses did so in the sight of the elders of israel.
english: Behold, I will stand before thee there upon the rock in Horeb; and thou shalt smite the rock, and there shall come water out of it, that the people may drink.’ And Moses did so in the sight of the elders of Israel.

searchText: exodus 17:7 and the name of the place was called massah, and meribah, because of the striving of the children of israel, and because they tried the lord, saying: ‘is the lord among us, or not?’
english: And the name of the place was called Massah, and Meribah, because of the striving of the children of Israel, and because they tried the LORD, saying: ‘Is the LORD among us, or not?’

searchText: exodus 17:8 then came amalek, and fought with israel in rephidim.
english: Then came Amalek, and fought with Israel in Rephidim.

searchText: exodus 17:9 and moses said unto joshua: ‘choose us out men, and go out, fight with amalek; tomorrow i will stand on the top of the hill with the rod of god in my hand.’
english: And Moses said unto Joshua: ‘Choose us out men, and go out, fight with Amalek; tomorrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the rod of God in my hand.’

searchText: exodus 17:10 so joshua did as moses had said to him, and fought with amalek; and moses, aaron, and hur went up to the top of the hill.
english: So Joshua did as Moses had said to him, and fought with Amalek; and Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.

searchText: exodus 17:11 and it came to pass, when moses held up his hand, that israel prevailed; and when he let down his hand, amalek prevailed.
english: And it came to pass, when Moses held up his hand, that Israel prevailed; and when he let down his hand, Amalek prevailed.

searchText: exodus 17:12 but moses’hands were heavy; and they took a stone, and put it under him, and he sat thereon; and aaron and hur stayed up his hands, the one on the one side, and the other on the other side; and his hands were steady until the going down of the sun.
english: But Moses’hands were heavy; and they took a stone, and put it under him, and he sat thereon; and Aaron and Hur stayed up his hands, the one on the one side, and the other on the other side; and his hands were steady until the going down of the sun.

searchText: exodus 17:13 and joshua discomfited amalek and his people with the edge of the sword.
english: And Joshua discomfited Amalek and his people with the edge of the sword.

searchText: exodus 17:14 and the lord said unto moses: ‘write this for a memorial in the book, and rehearse it in the ears of joshua: for i will utterly blot out the remembrance of amalek from under heaven.’
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Write this for a memorial in the book, and rehearse it in the ears of Joshua: for I will utterly blot out the remembrance of Amalek from under heaven.’

searchText: exodus 17:15 and moses built an altar, and called the name of it adonai-nissi.
english: And Moses built an altar, and called the name of it Adonai-nissi.

searchText: exodus 17:16 and he said: ‘the hand upon the throne of the lord: the lord will have war with amalek from generation to generation.’
english: And he said: ‘The hand upon the throne of the LORD: the LORD will have war with Amalek from generation to generation.’

searchText: exodus 18:1 now jethro, the priest of midian, moses’father-in-law, heard of all that god had done for moses, and for israel his people, how that the lord had brought israel out of egypt.
english: Now Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses’father-in-law, heard of all that God had done for Moses, and for Israel His people, how that the LORD had brought Israel out of Egypt.

searchText: exodus 18:2 and jethro, moses’father-in-law, took zipporah, moses’wife, after he had sent her away,
english: And Jethro, Moses’father-in-law, took Zipporah, Moses’wife, after he had sent her away,

searchText: exodus 18:3 and her two sons; of whom the name of the one was gershom; for he said: ‘i have been a stranger in a strange land’;
english: and her two sons; of whom the name of the one was Gershom; for he said: ‘I have been a stranger in a strange land’;

searchText: exodus 18:4 and the name of the other was eliezer: ‘for the god of my father was my help, and delivered me from the sword of pharaoh.’
english: and the name of the other was Eliezer: ‘for the God of my father was my help, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh.’

searchText: exodus 18:5 and jethro, moses’father-in-law, came with his sons and his wife unto moses into the wilderness where he was encamped, at the mount of god;
english: And Jethro, Moses’father-in-law, came with his sons and his wife unto Moses into the wilderness where he was encamped, at the mount of God;

searchText: exodus 18:6 and he said unto moses: ‘i thy father-in-law jethro am coming unto thee, and thy wife, and her two sons with her.’
english: and he said unto Moses: ‘I thy father-in-law Jethro am coming unto thee, and thy wife, and her two sons with her.’

searchText: exodus 18:7 and moses went out to meet his father-in-law, and bowed down and kissed him; and they asked each other of their welfare; and they came into the tent.
english: And Moses went out to meet his father-in-law, and bowed down and kissed him; and they asked each other of their welfare; and they came into the tent.

searchText: exodus 18:8 and moses told his father-in-law all that the lord had done unto pharaoh and to the egyptians for israel’s sake, all the travail that had come upon them by the way, and how the lord delivered them.
english: And Moses told his father-in-law all that the LORD had done unto Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel’s sake, all the travail that had come upon them by the way, and how the LORD delivered them.

searchText: exodus 18:9 and jethro rejoiced for all the goodness which the lord had done to israel, in that he had delivered them out of the hand of the egyptians.
english: And Jethro rejoiced for all the goodness which the LORD had done to Israel, in that He had delivered them out of the hand of the Egyptians.

searchText: exodus 18:10 and jethro said: ‘blessed be the lord, who hath delivered you out of the hand of the egyptians, and out of the hand of pharaoh; who hath delivered the people from under the hand of the egyptians.
english: And Jethro said: ‘Blessed be the LORD, who hath delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians, and out of the hand of Pharaoh; who hath delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians.

searchText: exodus 18:11 now i know that the lord is greater than all gods; yea, for that they dealt proudly against them.’
english: Now I know that the LORD is greater than all gods; yea, for that they dealt proudly against them.’

searchText: exodus 18:12 and jethro, moses’father-in-law, took a burnt-offering and sacrifices for god; and aaron came, and all the elders of israel, to eat bread with moses’father-in-law before god.
english: And Jethro, Moses’father-in-law, took a burnt-offering and sacrifices for God; and Aaron came, and all the elders of Israel, to eat bread with Moses’father-in-law before God.

searchText: exodus 18:13 and it came to pass on the morrow, that moses sat to judge the people; and the people stood about moses from the morning unto the evening.
english: And it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses sat to judge the people; and the people stood about Moses from the morning unto the evening.

searchText: exodus 18:14 and when moses’father-in-law saw all that he did to the people, he said: ‘what is this thing that thou doest to the people? why sittest thou thyself alone, and all the people stand about thee from morning unto even?’
english: And when Moses’father-in-law saw all that he did to the people, he said: ‘What is this thing that thou doest to the people? why sittest thou thyself alone, and all the people stand about thee from morning unto even?’

searchText: exodus 18:15 and moses said unto his father-in-law: ‘because the people come unto me to inquire of god;
english: And Moses said unto his father-in-law: ‘Because the people come unto me to inquire of God;

searchText: exodus 18:16 when they have a matter, it cometh unto me; and i judge between a man and his neighbour, and i make them know the statutes of god, and his laws.’
english: when they have a matter, it cometh unto me; and I judge between a man and his neighbour, and I make them know the statutes of God, and His laws.’

searchText: exodus 18:17 and moses’father-in-law said unto him: ‘the thing that thou doest is not good.
english: And Moses’father-in-law said unto him: ‘The thing that thou doest is not good.

searchText: exodus 18:18 thou wilt surely wear away, both thou, and this people that is with thee; for the thing is too heavy for thee; thou art not able to perform it thyself alone.
english: Thou wilt surely wear away, both thou, and this people that is with thee; for the thing is too heavy for thee; thou art not able to perform it thyself alone.

searchText: exodus 18:19 hearken now unto my voice, i will give thee counsel, and god be with thee: be thou for the people before god, and bring thou the causes unto god.
english: Hearken now unto my voice, I will give thee counsel, and God be with thee: be thou for the people before God, and bring thou the causes unto God.

searchText: exodus 18:20 and thou shalt teach them the statutes and the laws, and shalt show them the way wherein they must walk, and the work that they must do.
english: And thou shalt teach them the statutes and the laws, and shalt show them the way wherein they must walk, and the work that they must do.

searchText: exodus 18:21 moreover thou shalt provide out of all the people able men, such as fear god, men of truth, hating unjust gain; and place such over them, to be rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
english: Moreover thou shalt provide out of all the people able men, such as fear God, men of truth, hating unjust gain; and place such over them, to be rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.

searchText: exodus 18:22 and let them judge the people at all seasons; and it shall be, that every great matter they shall bring unto thee, but every small matter they shall judge themselves; so shall they make it easier for thee and bear the burden with thee.
english: And let them judge the people at all seasons; and it shall be, that every great matter they shall bring unto thee, but every small matter they shall judge themselves; so shall they make it easier for thee and bear the burden with thee.

searchText: exodus 18:23 if thou shalt do this thing, and god command thee so, then thou shalt be able to endure, and all this people also shall go to their place in peace.’
english: If thou shalt do this thing, and God command thee so, then thou shalt be able to endure, and all this people also shall go to their place in peace.’

searchText: exodus 18:24 so moses hearkened to the voice of his father-in-law, and did all that he had said.
english: So Moses hearkened to the voice of his father-in-law, and did all that he had said.

searchText: exodus 18:25 and moses chose able men out of all israel, and made them heads over the people, rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
english: And Moses chose able men out of all Israel, and made them heads over the people, rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.

searchText: exodus 18:26 and they judged the people at all seasons: the hard causes they brought unto moses, but every small matter they judged themselves.
english: And they judged the people at all seasons: the hard causes they brought unto Moses, but every small matter they judged themselves.

searchText: exodus 18:27 and moses let his father-in-law depart; and he went his way into his own land.
english: And Moses let his father-in-law depart; and he went his way into his own land.

searchText: exodus 19:1 in the third month after the children of israel were gone forth out of the land of egypt, the same day came they into the wilderness of sinai.
english: In the third month after the children of Israel were gone forth out of the land of Egypt, the same day came they into the wilderness of Sinai.

searchText: exodus 19:2 and when they were departed from rephidim, and were come to the wilderness of sinai, they encamped in the wilderness; and there israel encamped before the mount.
english: And when they were departed from Rephidim, and were come to the wilderness of Sinai, they encamped in the wilderness; and there Israel encamped before the mount.

searchText: exodus 19:3 and moses went up unto god, and the lord called unto him out of the mountain, saying: ‘thus shalt thou say to the house of jacob, and tell the children of israel:
english: And Moses went up unto God, and the LORD called unto him out of the mountain, saying: ‘Thus shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel:

searchText: exodus 19:4 ye have seen what i did unto the egyptians, and how i bore you on eagles’wings, and brought you unto myself.
english: Ye have seen what I did unto the Egyptians, and how I bore you on eagles’wings, and brought you unto Myself.

searchText: exodus 19:5 now therefore, if ye will hearken unto my voice indeed, and keep my covenant, then ye shall be mine own treasure from among all peoples; for all the earth is mine;
english: Now therefore, if ye will hearken unto My voice indeed, and keep My covenant, then ye shall be Mine own treasure from among all peoples; for all the earth is Mine;

searchText: exodus 19:6 and ye shall be unto me a kingdom of priests, and a holy nation. these are the words which thou shalt speak unto the children of israel.’
english: and ye shall be unto Me a kingdom of priests, and a holy nation. These are the words which thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel.’

searchText: exodus 19:7 and moses came and called for the elders of the people, and set before them all these words which the lord commanded him.
english: And Moses came and called for the elders of the people, and set before them all these words which the LORD commanded him.

searchText: exodus 19:8 and all the people answered together, and said: ‘all that the lord hath spoken we will do.’ and moses reported the words of the people unto the lord.
english: And all the people answered together, and said: ‘All that the LORD hath spoken we will do.’ And Moses reported the words of the people unto the LORD.

searchText: exodus 19:9 and the lord said unto moses: ‘lo, i come unto thee in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when i speak with thee, and may also believe thee for ever.’ and moses told the words of the people unto the lord.
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Lo, I come unto thee in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with thee, and may also believe thee for ever.’ And Moses told the words of the people unto the LORD.

searchText: exodus 19:10 and the lord said unto moses: ‘go unto the people, and sanctify them to-day and to-morrow, and let them wash their garments,
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Go unto the people, and sanctify them to-day and to-morrow, and let them wash their garments,

searchText: exodus 19:11 and be ready against the third day; for the third day the lord will come down in the sight of all the people upon mount sinai.
english: and be ready against the third day; for the third day the LORD will come down in the sight of all the people upon mount Sinai.

searchText: exodus 19:12 and thou shalt set bounds unto the people round about, saying: take heed to yourselves, that ye go not up into the mount, or touch the border of it; whosoever toucheth the mount shall be surely put to death;
english: And thou shalt set bounds unto the people round about, saying: Take heed to yourselves, that ye go not up into the mount, or touch the border of it; whosoever toucheth the mount shall be surely put to death;

searchText: exodus 19:13 no hand shall touch him, but he shall surely be stoned, or shot through; whether it be beast or man, it shall not live; when the ram’s horn soundeth long, they shall come up to the mount.’
english: no hand shall touch him, but he shall surely be stoned, or shot through; whether it be beast or man, it shall not live; when the ram’s horn soundeth long, they shall come up to the mount.’

searchText: exodus 19:14 and moses went down from the mount unto the people, and sanctified the people; and they washed their garments.
english: And Moses went down from the mount unto the people, and sanctified the people; and they washed their garments.

searchText: exodus 19:15 and he said unto the people: ‘be ready against the third day; come not near a woman.’
english: And he said unto the people: ‘Be ready against the third day; come not near a woman.’

searchText: exodus 19:16 and it came to pass on the third day, when it was morning, that there were thunders and lightnings and a thick cloud upon the mount, and the voice of a horn exceeding loud; and all the people that were in the camp trembled.
english: And it came to pass on the third day, when it was morning, that there were thunders and lightnings and a thick cloud upon the mount, and the voice of a horn exceeding loud; and all the people that were in the camp trembled.

searchText: exodus 19:17 and moses brought forth the people out of the camp to meet god; and they stood at the nether part of the mount.
english: And Moses brought forth the people out of the camp to meet God; and they stood at the nether part of the mount.

searchText: exodus 19:18 now mount sinai was altogether on smoke, because the lord descended upon it in fire; and the smoke thereof ascended as the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mount quaked greatly.
english: Now mount Sinai was altogether on smoke, because the LORD descended upon it in fire; and the smoke thereof ascended as the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mount quaked greatly.

searchText: exodus 19:19 and when the voice of the horn waxed louder and louder, moses spoke, and god answered him by a voice.
english: And when the voice of the horn waxed louder and louder, Moses spoke, and God answered him by a voice.

searchText: exodus 19:20 and the lord came down upon mount sinai, to the top of the mount; and the lord called moses to the top of the mount; and moses went up.
english: And the LORD came down upon mount Sinai, to the top of the mount; and the LORD called Moses to the top of the mount; and Moses went up.

searchText: exodus 19:21 and the lord said unto moses: ‘go down, charge the people, lest they break through unto the lord to gaze, and many of them perish.
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘Go down, charge the people, lest they break through unto the LORD to gaze, and many of them perish.

searchText: exodus 19:22 and let the priests also, that come near to the lord, sanctify themselves, lest the lord break forth upon them.’
english: And let the priests also, that come near to the LORD, sanctify themselves, lest the LORD break forth upon them.’

searchText: exodus 19:23 and moses said unto the lord: ‘the people cannot come up to mount sinai; for thou didst charge us, saying: set bounds about the mount, and sanctify it.’
english: And Moses said unto the LORD: ‘The people cannot come up to mount Sinai; for thou didst charge us, saying: Set bounds about the mount, and sanctify it.’

searchText: exodus 19:24 and the lord said unto him: ‘go, get thee down, and thou shalt come up, thou, and aaron with thee; but let not the priests and the people break through to come up unto the lord, lest he break forth upon them.’
english: And the LORD said unto him: ‘Go, get thee down, and thou shalt come up, thou, and Aaron with thee; but let not the priests and the people break through to come up unto the LORD, lest He break forth upon them.’

searchText: exodus 19:25 so moses went down unto the people, and told them.
english: So Moses went down unto the people, and told them.

searchText: exodus 20:1 and god spoke all these words, saying:
english: And God spoke all these words, saying:

searchText: exodus 20:2 i am the lord thy god, who brought thee out of the land of egypt, out of the house of bondage.
english: I am the LORD thy God, who brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

searchText: exodus 20:3 thou shalt have no other gods before me.
english: Thou shalt have no other gods before Me.

searchText: exodus 20:4 thou shalt not make unto thee a graven image, nor any manner of likeness, of any thing that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth;
english: Thou shalt not make unto thee a graven image, nor any manner of likeness, of any thing that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth;

searchText: exodus 20:5 thou shalt not bow down unto them, nor serve them; for i the lord thy god am a jealous god, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation of them that hate me;
english: thou shalt not bow down unto them, nor serve them; for I the LORD thy God am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation of them that hate Me;

searchText: exodus 20:6 and showing mercy unto the thousandth generation of them that love me and keep my commandments.
english: and showing mercy unto the thousandth generation of them that love Me and keep My commandments.

searchText: exodus 20:7 thou shalt not take the name of the lord thy god in vain; for the lord will not hold him guiltless that taketh his name in vain.
english: Thou shalt not take the name of the LORD thy God in vain; for the LORD will not hold him guiltless that taketh His name in vain.

searchText: exodus 20:8 remember the sabbath day, to keep it holy.
english: Remember the sabbath day, to keep it holy.

searchText: exodus 20:9 six days shalt thou labour, and do all thy work;
english: Six days shalt thou labour, and do all thy work;

searchText: exodus 20:10 but the seventh day is a sabbath unto the lord thy god, in it thou shalt not do any manner of work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, nor thy man-servant, nor thy maid-servant, nor thy cattle, nor thy stranger that is within thy gates;
english: but the seventh day is a sabbath unto the LORD thy God, in it thou shalt not do any manner of work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, nor thy man-servant, nor thy maid-servant, nor thy cattle, nor thy stranger that is within thy gates;

searchText: exodus 20:11 for in six days the lord made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that in them is, and rested on the seventh day; wherefore the lord blessed the sabbath day, and hallowed it.
english: for in six days the LORD made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that in them is, and rested on the seventh day; wherefore the LORD blessed the sabbath day, and hallowed it.

searchText: exodus 20:12 honour thy father and thy mother, that thy days may be long upon the land which the lord thy god giveth thee.
english: Honour thy father and thy mother, that thy days may be long upon the land which the LORD thy God giveth thee.

searchText: exodus 20:13 thou shalt not murder. thou shalt not commit adultery. thou shalt not steal. thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.
english: Thou shalt not murder. Thou shalt not commit adultery. Thou shalt not steal. Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.

searchText: exodus 20:14 thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s house; thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s wife, nor his man-servant, nor his maid-servant, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor any thing that is thy neighbour’s.
english: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s house; thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s wife, nor his man-servant, nor his maid-servant, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor any thing that is thy neighbour’s.

searchText: exodus 20:15 and all the people perceived the thunderings, and the lightnings, and the voice of the horn, and the mountain smoking; and when the people saw it, they trembled, and stood afar off.
english: And all the people perceived the thunderings, and the lightnings, and the voice of the horn, and the mountain smoking; and when the people saw it, they trembled, and stood afar off.

searchText: exodus 20:16 and they said unto moses: ‘speak thou with us, and we will hear; but let not god speak with us, lest we die.’
english: And they said unto Moses: ‘Speak thou with us, and we will hear; but let not God speak with us, lest we die.’

searchText: exodus 20:17 and moses said unto the people: ‘fear not; for god is come to prove you, and that his fear may be before you, that ye sin not.’
english: And Moses said unto the people: ‘Fear not; for God is come to prove you, and that His fear may be before you, that ye sin not.’

searchText: exodus 20:18 and the people stood afar off; but moses drew near unto the thick darkness where god was.
english: And the people stood afar off; but Moses drew near unto the thick darkness where God was.

searchText: exodus 20:19 and the lord said unto moses: thus thou shalt say unto the children of israel: ye yourselves have seen that i have talked with you from heaven.
english: And the LORD said unto Moses: Thus thou shalt say unto the children of Israel: Ye yourselves have seen that I have talked with you from heaven.

searchText: exodus 20:20 ye shall not make with me—gods of silver, or gods of gold, ye shall not make unto you.
english: Ye shall not make with Me—gods of silver, or gods of gold, ye shall not make unto you.

searchText: exodus 20:21 an altar of earth thou shalt make unto me, and shalt sacrifice thereon thy burnt-offerings, and thy peace-offerings, thy sheep, and thine oxen; in every place where i cause my name to be mentioned i will come unto thee and bless thee.
english: An altar of earth thou shalt make unto Me, and shalt sacrifice thereon thy burnt-offerings, and thy peace-offerings, thy sheep, and thine oxen; in every place where I cause My name to be mentioned I will come unto thee and bless thee.

searchText: exodus 20:22 and if thou make me an altar of stone, thou shalt not build it of hewn stones; for if thou lift up thy tool upon it, thou hast profaned it.
english: And if thou make Me an altar of stone, thou shalt not build it of hewn stones; for if thou lift up thy tool upon it, thou hast profaned it.

searchText: exodus 20:23 neither shalt thou go up by steps unto mine altar, that thy nakedness be not uncovered thereon.
english: Neither shalt thou go up by steps unto Mine altar, that thy nakedness be not uncovered thereon.

searchText: exodus 21:1 now these are the ordinances which thou shalt set before them.
english: Now these are the ordinances which thou shalt set before them.

searchText: exodus 21:2 if thou buy a hebrew servant, six years he shall serve; and in the seventh he shall go out free for nothing.
english: If thou buy a Hebrew servant, six years he shall serve; and in the seventh he shall go out free for nothing.

searchText: exodus 21:3 if he come in by himself, he shall go out by himself; if he be married, then his wife shall go out with him.
english: If he come in by himself, he shall go out by himself; if he be married, then his wife shall go out with him.

searchText: exodus 21:4 if his master give him a wife, and she bear him sons or daughters; the wife and her children shall be her master’s, and he shall go out by himself.
english: If his master give him a wife, and she bear him sons or daughters; the wife and her children shall be her master’s, and he shall go out by himself.

searchText: exodus 21:5 but if the servant shall plainly say: i love my master, my wife, and my children; i will not go out free;
english: But if the servant shall plainly say: I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free;

searchText: exodus 21:6 then his master shall bring him unto god, and shall bring him to the door, or unto the door-post; and his master shall bore his ear through with an awl; and he shall serve him for ever.
english: then his master shall bring him unto God, and shall bring him to the door, or unto the door-post; and his master shall bore his ear through with an awl; and he shall serve him for ever.

searchText: exodus 21:7 and if a man sell his daughter to be a maid-servant, she shall not go out as the men-servants do.
english: And if a man sell his daughter to be a maid-servant, she shall not go out as the men-servants do.

searchText: exodus 21:8 if she please not her master, who hath espoused her to himself, then shall he let her be redeemed; to sell her unto a foreign people he shall have no power, seeing he hath dealt deceitfully with her.
english: If she please not her master, who hath espoused her to himself, then shall he let her be redeemed; to sell her unto a foreign people he shall have no power, seeing he hath dealt deceitfully with her.

searchText: exodus 21:9 and if he espouse her unto his son, he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters.
english: And if he espouse her unto his son, he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters.

searchText: exodus 21:10 if he take him another wife, her food, her raiment, and her conjugal rights, shall he not diminish.
english: If he take him another wife, her food, her raiment, and her conjugal rights, shall he not diminish.

searchText: exodus 21:11 and if he do not these three unto her, then shall she go out for nothing, without money.
english: And if he do not these three unto her, then shall she go out for nothing, without money.

searchText: exodus 21:12 he that smiteth a man, so that he dieth, shall surely be put to death.
english: He that smiteth a man, so that he dieth, shall surely be put to death.

searchText: exodus 21:13 and if a man lie not in wait, but god cause it to come to hand; then i will appoint thee a place whither he may flee.
english: And if a man lie not in wait, but God cause it to come to hand; then I will appoint thee a place whither he may flee.

searchText: exodus 21:14 and if a man come presumptuously upon his neighbour, to slay him with guile; thou shalt take him from mine altar, that he may die.
english: And if a man come presumptuously upon his neighbour, to slay him with guile; thou shalt take him from Mine altar, that he may die.

searchText: exodus 21:15 and he that smiteth his father, or his mother, shall be surely put to death. .
english: And he that smiteth his father, or his mother, shall be surely put to death. .

searchText: exodus 21:16 and he that stealeth a man, and selleth him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.
english: And he that stealeth a man, and selleth him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.

searchText: exodus 21:17 and he that curseth his father or his mother, shall surely be put to death.
english: And he that curseth his father or his mother, shall surely be put to death.

searchText: exodus 21:18 and if men contend, and one smite the other with a stone, or with his fist, and he die not, but keep his bed;
english: And if men contend, and one smite the other with a stone, or with his fist, and he die not, but keep his bed;

searchText: exodus 21:19 if he rise again, and walk abroad upon his staff, then shall he that smote him be quit; only he shall pay for the loss of his time, and shall cause him to be thoroughly healed.
english: if he rise again, and walk abroad upon his staff, then shall he that smote him be quit; only he shall pay for the loss of his time, and shall cause him to be thoroughly healed.

searchText: exodus 21:20 and if a man smite his bondman, or his bondwoman, with a rod, and he die under his hand, he shall surely be punished.
english: And if a man smite his bondman, or his bondwoman, with a rod, and he die under his hand, he shall surely be punished.

searchText: exodus 21:21 notwithstanding if he continue a day or two, he shall not be punished; for he is his money.
english: Notwithstanding if he continue a day or two, he shall not be punished; for he is his money.

searchText: exodus 21:22 and if men strive together, and hurt a woman with child, so that her fruit depart, and yet no harm follow, he shall be surely fined, according as the woman’s husband shall lay upon him; and he shall pay as the judges determine.
english: And if men strive together, and hurt a woman with child, so that her fruit depart, and yet no harm follow, he shall be surely fined, according as the woman’s husband shall lay upon him; and he shall pay as the judges determine.

searchText: exodus 21:23 but if any harm follow, then thou shalt give life for life,
english: But if any harm follow, then thou shalt give life for life,

searchText: exodus 21:24 eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
english: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,

searchText: exodus 21:25 burning for burning, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.
english: burning for burning, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.

searchText: exodus 21:26 and if a man smite the eye of his bondman, or the eye of his bondwoman, and destroy it, he shall let him go free for his eye’s sake.
english: And if a man smite the eye of his bondman, or the eye of his bondwoman, and destroy it, he shall let him go free for his eye’s sake.

searchText: exodus 21:27 and if he smite out his bondman’s tooth, or his bondwoman’s tooth, he shall let him go free for his tooth’s sake.
english: And if he smite out his bondman’s tooth, or his bondwoman’s tooth, he shall let him go free for his tooth’s sake.

searchText: exodus 21:28 and if an ox gore a man or a woman, that they die, the ox shall be surely stoned, and its flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall be quit.
english: And if an ox gore a man or a woman, that they die, the ox shall be surely stoned, and its flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall be quit.

searchText: exodus 21:29 but if the ox was wont to gore in time past, and warning hath been given to its owner, and he hath not kept it in, but it hath killed a man or a woman; the ox shall be stoned, and its owner also shall be put to death.
english: But if the ox was wont to gore in time past, and warning hath been given to its owner, and he hath not kept it in, but it hath killed a man or a woman; the ox shall be stoned, and its owner also shall be put to death.

searchText: exodus 21:30 if there be laid on him a ransom, then he shall give for the redemption of his life whatsoever is laid upon him.
english: If there be laid on him a ransom, then he shall give for the redemption of his life whatsoever is laid upon him.

searchText: exodus 21:31 whether it have gored a son, or have gored a daughter, according to this judgment shall it be done unto him.
english: Whether it have gored a son, or have gored a daughter, according to this judgment shall it be done unto him.

searchText: exodus 21:32 if the ox gore a bondman or a bondwoman, he shall give unto their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.
english: If the ox gore a bondman or a bondwoman, he shall give unto their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.

searchText: exodus 21:33 and if a man shall open a pit, or if a man shall dig a pit and not cover it, and an ox or an ass fall therein,
english: And if a man shall open a pit, or if a man shall dig a pit and not cover it, and an ox or an ass fall therein,

searchText: exodus 21:34 the owner of the pit shall make it good; he shall give money unto the owner of them, and the dead beast shall be his.
english: the owner of the pit shall make it good; he shall give money unto the owner of them, and the dead beast shall be his.

searchText: exodus 21:35 and if one man’s ox hurt another’s, so that it dieth; then they shall sell the live ox, and divide the price of it; and the dead also they shall divide.
english: And if one man’s ox hurt another’s, so that it dieth; then they shall sell the live ox, and divide the price of it; and the dead also they shall divide.

searchText: exodus 21:36 or if it be known that the ox was wont to gore in time past, and its owner hath not kept it in; he shall surely pay ox for ox, and the dead beast shall be his own.
english: Or if it be known that the ox was wont to gore in time past, and its owner hath not kept it in; he shall surely pay ox for ox, and the dead beast shall be his own.

searchText: exodus 21:37 if a man steal an ox, or a sheep, and kill it, or sell it, he shall pay five oxen for an ox, and four sheep for a sheep.
english: If a man steal an ox, or a sheep, and kill it, or sell it, he shall pay five oxen for an ox, and four sheep for a sheep.

searchText: exodus 22:1 if a thief be found breaking in, and be smitten so that he dieth, there shall be no bloodguiltiness for him.
english: If a thief be found breaking in, and be smitten so that he dieth, there shall be no bloodguiltiness for him.

searchText: exodus 22:2 if the sun be risen upon him, there shall be bloodguiltiness for him—he shall make restitution; if he have nothing, then he shall be sold for his theft.
english: If the sun be risen upon him, there shall be bloodguiltiness for him—he shall make restitution; if he have nothing, then he shall be sold for his theft.

searchText: exodus 22:3 if the theft be found in his hand alive, whether it be ox, or ass, or sheep, he shall pay double.
english: If the theft be found in his hand alive, whether it be ox, or ass, or sheep, he shall pay double.

searchText: exodus 22:4 if a man cause a field or vineyard to be eaten, and shall let his beast loose, and it feed in another man’s field; of the best of his own field, and of the best of his own vineyard, shall he make restitution.
